En: They're too poor to have a phone line.
Zh: 他们穷的连电话线都买不起。

En: Maurice, what?! a woman yells. Then: "Yes-yes, okay, if it was the police..." She starts explaining something.
Zh: “莫里斯，什么？！”一个女人在大喊。然后：*是的是的，好吧，如果是警察……*她开始解释着什么。

En: Good. You have a name now.
Zh: 很好。现在你找到一个名字了。

En: And then goes for a little drink later, on the lookout...
Zh: 然后可以去小喝一杯，在瞭望台……

En: Marie... A moment passes.
Zh: “玛丽……”过了一会儿。

En: On Meroe drive in Central Jamrock -- in a darkened hall lit by orange desk lamps -- far away from the noise outside -- a middle-aged man taps commands into an old radiocomputer. A printout falls on the desk. Behind him, a lonely reader scours some dusty bookshelves, looking for a paperback...
Zh: 加姆洛克中心区的麦罗埃街——在一个被橙色灯光照亮的黑色大厅——远离外面的杂音——某个中年男子在正对着一台老旧的无线电脑输入指令。桌子上放着一份打印资料。在他身后，一个孤独的读者正在擦拭满是灰尘的书架，寻找一本平装书……

En: That's where the smoker on the balcony lives, isn't it?
Zh: 那就是阳台上的吸烟者住的地方，不是吗？

En: By the pier, north of here. Those big apartment buildings there.
Zh: 就在码头附近，这里的北面。那些大公寓楼。

En: I'm looking for any information that you can provide on Billie Méjean, a reader.
Zh: “关于你们的一个读者，比莉·梅让，我需要你们能够提供的所有信息。”

En: Billie, Billie *Méjean*, you said? Give me a moment, I'll have to check our database. He puts down the receiver.
Zh: “比莉，你说，比莉·*梅让*？给我几分钟，我检查一下资料库。”他放下听筒。

En: Yes, hello, are you still there? You can hear him fiddle with the printout. "I found Billie Méjean's home address, is that alright? No phone number unfortunately."
Zh: “有了，喂喂，请问还在吗？”你听见他翻动资料的声音。“我找到了比莉·梅让的家庭住址，可以吗？可惜没有电话号码。”

En: Yes, home address is fine.
Zh: “可以，家庭住址就行。”

En: Here we go, sir: Rue de Saint-Ghislaine 33B, apartment no 20. It's in Martinaise, I believe... Capeside Apartments, it says. That's all.
Zh: “那我就说了，先生：圣·吉斯莱恩路33B，20号公寓。我相信它就在马丁内斯……上面写着岬岸公寓。就这些。”

En: I'm not really that good with addresses.
Zh: “我真的不太擅长记地址。”

En: Maybe you can write it down? It's Rue de Saint-Ghislaine 33B, apartment no 20. It's in Martinaise, I believe... Capeside Apartments, it says. That's all.
Zh: “也许你可以写下来？圣·吉斯莱恩路33B，20号公寓。我相信它就在马丁内斯……上面写着岬岸公寓。就这些。”

En: Do you have any other information on Billie Méjean?
Zh: “还有比莉·梅让的其他信息吗？”

En: Thank you. That's all from me, I have no other questions. (End call with the library.)
Zh: “谢谢。我可以了，没有其他问题。”（结束跟图书馆的通话。）

En: It says here that they returned their last book just a few days ago, but I wasn't at work that day.
Zh: “这上面写着他们几天前刚还了最后一本书，但是那天我没上班。”

En: Happy we could help. Good bye, officer. The librarian hangs up and the call gets redirected back to the station.
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你。再见，警官。”图书管理员挂断电话，电话又接回了警局。

En: Anything else you need from me? Alice asks.
Zh: “还有什么需要我帮忙的吗？”爱丽丝问到。

En: Do you know someone who was?
Zh: “你知道是谁吗？”

En: Marie? He covers the phone with his hand and yells out into the room behind him: "Marie! Do you remember a reader named Billie Méjean? They returned a Thibault book the other day..." You hear someone answer from afar.
Zh: “玛丽？”他用手捂住电话，然后朝着身后的房间大喊：“玛丽！你还记不记得一个叫比莉·梅让的读者？他们前几天来还过蒂博的书……”你听到远处有人回答的声音。

En: Loos, Radio City? That's the same book the working class woman was reading at the bookstand...
Zh: 卢斯，无线之城？书摊那个女工人看的就是这本书……

En: Something unpleasant twitches in your stomach.
Zh: 你的胃里有些不舒服的东西在抽搐。

En: Yes, it was my colleague Marie, the librarian is speaking into the phone again. "She said that it was Billie's *husband* who returned the book. He also asked for this new sci-fi release "Loos, Radio City '87", but we don't have it yet."
Zh: “没错，是我的同事玛丽，”图书管理员的声音再次响起。“她说来还书的是比莉的*丈夫*。他还想借一本新的科幻小说“卢斯，无线之城87”，但是我们这里还没到。”

En: So Billie Méjean is a woman, not a man? How did your colleague know that it was her husband?
Zh: “所以比莉·梅让是个女人，不是男人？你的同事怎么知道是她的丈夫？”

En: Can Marie describe to me what the husband looked like?
Zh: “玛丽能描述一下那位丈夫什么样吗？”

En: Marie knows Billie, she's been working here longer than me. Sometimes her husband returns some books for her.
Zh: “玛丽认识比莉，她在这里工作的时间比我长。有时候她的丈夫会代替她来还书。”

En: She said it was an older man... And that she's pretty sure he had had a drink or two the last time she saw him.
Zh: “她说是个年纪比较大的男人……而且她很肯定最后一次见到他的时候，对方肯定喝了一两杯。”

En: Thank you. That's all from me, I have no other questions. (End call with the library.)
Zh: “谢谢。我可以了，没有其他问题。”（结束跟图书馆的通话。）

En: Happy we could help. Good bye, officer. The librarian hangs up and the call gets redirected back to the station with a soft click...
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你。再见，警官。”图书管理员挂断电话，伴随一阵轻柔的咔嗒声，电话又重新接回了警局……

En: Anything else you need from me?
Zh: “还有什么需要我帮忙的吗？”

En: What was he wearing?
Zh: “他什么打扮？”

En: Uh, one second... The librarian turns away from the phone again and relays the question.
Zh: “呃，等一下……”图书管理员又离开话机，转述问题去了。

En: Sorry, Marie wasn't really paying any attention to that.
Zh: “抱歉，玛丽真的没有注意。”

En: Do you know the husband's name?
Zh: “你知道丈夫的名字吗？”

En: Sorry, no -- Marie only knows him by sight.
Zh: “抱歉，不知道——玛丽只是脸熟。”

En: You're not surprised? Okay. Anything else?
Zh: “你不吃惊吗？好吧。还有什么吗？”

En: This is *not* about self-immolation in the Samarskilt AR.
Zh: 这跟在萨弗里AR自焚*无关*。

En: Of course, yes. You were having health trouble.
Zh: 当然了，没错。你有健康方面的问题。

En: He sounds very careless about it all. Positively breezy.
Zh: 听上去他对这一切都不关心。明显轻松又愉快。

En: You're detecting very little in the way of empathy from Dr. Gottlieb.
Zh: 你没能从戈特利布医生那里感觉到什么同理心。

En: Abstinence *could* have immediate positive effects on your health.
Zh: 节制*可以*立刻对你的健康产生积极的影响。

En: It certainly does not help with aiming...
Zh: 确实对瞄准没什么帮助……

En: Accept it. The body is an object and objects break down. Do what good you can with yours, before the rest goes too.
Zh: 接受吧。身体是一个物体，是物体就会出故障。在其他部分坏掉之前，先尽你所能好好利用吧。

En: Sort of. But by god does it *burn* in your chest...
Zh: 有点吧。天啦，但是它在你的胸口*燃烧*……

En: There is no surprise in his voice. Only careless superiority.
Zh: 他的声音没有丝毫的惊讶。只有无忧无虑的优越性。

En: It's hard to say if he doesn't believe you -- or doesn't care.
Zh: 很难说他是不相信——还是不关心。

En: And no, I *don't* want to hear a *political commentary* on the topic. In fact -- I've got work to do. Some idiot has glued his eyelids shut with Cyanoacrylate. It looks like Mack Torson.
Zh: “不，我*不想*听到那个话题的*政治评论*。事实上——我还有工作要做。有个白痴用氰基丙烯酸盐把自己的眼皮黏在一起了。看起来像是麦克·托森。”

En: It's not fucking cryo-actilate... it's super glue, doc.
Zh: “不是他妈的什么氰基丙烯酸盐……是超级粘胶，医生。”

En: Cyanoacrylate *is* super glue.
Zh: 氰基丙烯酸盐*就是*超级粘胶。

En: The money is probably going to some old, oily...
Zh: 金钱大概都流向了那帮年迈的，油腻的人兜里吧……

En: Doc, someone broke my heart.
Zh: “医生，有人伤了我的心。”

En: Wait... pumped and dumped?
Zh: “等等……热恋然后被甩？”

En: Do you know who this person was, have I told you...
Zh: “你知道那个人是谁吗，我告诉过你的那个……”

En: That's what the guys say, officer. That some chick pumped you -- and then dumped you. Threw you away like an empty juice carton.
Zh: “是那些家伙说的，警官。说有个小妞勾搭你——然后甩了你。把你当成个空果汁盒给扔掉了。”

En: You must have me confused for a close personal friend... I am not. I am a medical professional -- with a constant *idiot* emergency on my hands.
Zh: “你一定是把我跟某个亲密朋友搞混了吧……我不是。我是个医学专家——手上总会遇到*白痴*的紧急事故。”

En: Don't take it personally. There just wasn't any *juice* left in your carton, pal.
Zh: 别往心里去。并不是你的盒子里就没有*果汁*了，老兄。

En: You hear someone whine in the background. "My eye, doc... my eye..."
Zh: 你听见背景里有人在抱怨。“医生，我的眼睛……我的眼睛……”

En: One needs tending to right now, he thinks, looking around the room. God, these apes...
Zh: 又有人要照顾了，他四下打量着房间，默默想到。天啦，这群类人猿……

En: So it's political! You're being *neglected* because of political reasons...
Zh: 所以的确跟政治有关！因为政治上的原因，你被*忽视*了……

En: No wonder I'm in so much pain. I feel like I've been... damaged.
Zh: “难怪我觉得这么疼。我感觉自己已经……坏掉了。”

En: Ignorance is bliss, as they say. Go ahead, answer the man!
Zh: 大家都说，无知便是福。快啊，回答那个男人。

En: Yes. Probably in more ways than one.
Zh: “是的。也许不止一种方式。”

En: You want the real, honest-to-god truth? Stop drinking, eat magnesium and vitamin D. Our station is not a retirement home. We don't have the funds to deal with *rock stars* past their prime.
Zh: “你想要真真正正、毫无隐瞒的真相吗？那就别喝酒，吃点镁片和维他命D。我们的警局不是养老院。我们没有资金安置已经过了黄金时期的*摇滚明星*。”

En: Anything else I can help you with, officer?
Zh: “还有什么能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: She seems to hesitate. "Yes, officer, you will be connected in a moment..."
Zh: 她好像有点犹豫。“是的，警官，马上为你接通……”

En: It will take just a moment, officer...
Zh: “稍微等一下，警官……”

En: Her voices fades out into the familiar radio static.
Zh: 她的声音消失在熟悉的无线电杂音中。

En: You hear a man clearing his throat briskly, then an answer: "Gottlieb. What'd ya want?"
Zh: 你听见一个男人快速清了清喉咙，然后回答到：“我是戈特利布。请问有什么事？”

En: Gottlieb. What'd you get yourself into this time? The question is followed by the sound of someone taking a long drag.
Zh: “我是戈特利布。这次你又惹上什么麻烦了？”发问之后，紧接着是某人抽了一大口烟的声音。

En: How did you know it was me?
Zh: “你怎么知道是我？”

En: I've been shot...
Zh: “我中枪了……”

En: I've been bad and I want someone to abuse me.
Zh: “我很糟糕，想找人骂我一顿。”

En: Didn't. But now I do. How's the drinking going?
Zh: “不知道。不过现在知道了。喝的怎么样了？”

En: I decided to stop. Completely. Forever.
Zh: “我决定戒掉。彻底的。永久的。”

En: I'm trying really, really hard not to.
Zh: “我真的很努力，很努力地控制我自己。”

En: It's going great! I feel like it's really helping me with this case!
Zh: “挺好的！我觉得对我的案子真的很有帮助！”

En: Great to hear it. Stop by some time when you're done with your case -- I'll draw some blood, run it through -- let you know how much 'forever' your liver's going to give you.
Zh: “很高兴听到这个消息。案子处理完之后，抽个时间来看看——我会抽点血，检查一下——让你知道你的‘肝脏’还能给你多少‘永远’。”

En: Great. Have a medal. I have an autopsy to attend to. An autopsy of one Chester McLaine, who died of *fish poisoning*.
Zh: “很好，都能得奖章了。我要去验尸了。一个叫切斯特·麦克莱恩的人，死于*鱼类中毒*。”

En: Fucking... I'm not dead, it just hurts, okay?
Zh: “去你妈的……我没死，只是有点疼，好吗？”

En: Ah, it's you. Walking, talking, though?
Zh: “啊，是你啊。还能走路，能说话？”

En: Yes, but...
Zh: “是的，不过……”

En: Great! How's the drinking going?
Zh: “太好了！你喝的怎么样了？”

En: Oh, it's you. I know a woman skilled at that sort of thing over in Jamrock... but I wouldn't want to inflict you upon her. Best of luck, detective.
Zh: “哦，是你啊。我知道加姆洛克有个女人，特别精通这种事情……但我不想把你强行推到她面前。祝你好运了，警探。”

En: I guess that's it for now. [Hang up.]
Zh: “那就先这样吧。”[挂断。]

En: Mhphm. The phone clicks. Suddenly you hear the already familiar voice...
Zh: “嗯。”电话咔哒响了一声。突然间，你听到一个很熟悉的声音……

En: You know who I am?
Zh: “你知道我是谁？”

En: I think I've developed extreme photosensitivity...
Zh: “我想我已经开发出了极强的感光灵敏度……”

En: I think I've had a heart attack...
Zh: “我想我心脏病发作了……”

En: Gottlieb. What is it now? There's not the slightest hint of wanting to know the answer in that familiar raspy voice.
Zh: “我是戈特利布。现在又怎么了？”在那熟悉又刺耳的声音里，没有表露出丝毫想知道答案的迹象。

En: He's carelessly chewing on a piece of hard candy.
Zh: 他漫不经心地咀嚼着一块硬糖。

En: I don't want to talk about this anymore.
Zh: “我不想再提这个了。”

En: Great. I have an autopsy to attend to. An autopsy of one Chester McLaine, who died of *fish poisoning*.
Zh: “很好。我要去验尸了。一个叫切斯特·麦克莱恩的人，死于*鱼类中毒*。”

En: I was told to call the lazareth. People are worried about me...
Zh: “有人叫我打给检疫所。大家很担心我……”

En: I'm in a bad way, doc. A real *bad* way.
Zh: “我的情况很糟糕，医生。真的*很糟糕*。”

En: Just a routine check-up.
Zh: “只是例行检查。”

En: Oh, the man is unpleasantly surprised. "It's you."
Zh: “哦，”男人很惊讶，但不是开心的那种。“是你。”

En: Was that a snarl or a suppressed groan? No love for you here.
Zh: 是在咆哮，还是压抑的呻吟？这里对你可没多少爱。

En: Yes, there's no end to the misfortunes fate has seen fit to rain upon me.
Zh: “是的，你是我注定要不断承受不幸的命运。”

En: Good fortune, you mean good fortune, right? You're basking in the floodlight of my glory.
Zh: “好运，你是说好运，对吗？你正沐浴在我耀眼的荣光之下。”

En: Yes, we live in difficult times. But at least it's over. Almost over, that is...
Zh: “是的，我们生活在艰难的时期。但是至少已经结束了。就快结束了……”

En: I'm sorry I've been such a bother to you...
Zh: “对不起，我给你添了这么多麻烦……”

En: I don't know anything about fate. I just wanted to talk about a medical issue.
Zh: “我不知道什么命运。我只想聊一个医学问题。”

En: Sure, sure -- but can you tell me about myself? As in, who I am?
Zh: “当然，当然了——但是你能说说我吗？比如，我是谁？”

En: *Who* you are? You lost your human visage a while back. Now let's get on with it -- I've got more important things to do.
Zh: “你是*谁*？不久之前你刚刚失去了人类的面貌。现在我们继续吧——我还有很重要的事要做。”

En: Then let's get on with it. Clock is ticking.
Zh: “那我们就继续吧。时间不等人。”

En: Oh, spare me the sad drunken blubbering and get on with it. What's your complaint?
Zh: “哦，别又给我来那套，喝得醉醺醺的，又哭又闹，赶紧继续吧。你要抱怨什么？”

En: For you it probably is. I'm counting on my lungs outliving your liver by a wide margin. He pauses. "Not that that's anything to fête."
Zh: “对于你来说是可能的。我还指望自己的肺比你的肝脏多活很久呢。”他停顿了一下。“这并不是什么值得庆祝的事。”

En: Was he speaking about his longer life or your shorter one when he said it was no cause for celebration?
Zh: 他说不值得庆祝的时候，是在说他会活得比较久，还是你活不长？

En: Basking in your glory, yeah. Practically drowning in it. Please, tell me what your complaint is before I am completely submerged.
Zh: “沐浴在你的荣光里，是啊。实际上都快溺死了。麻烦你，在我还没完全沉下去之前，赶紧说说你要抱怨什么。”

En: A bit of both. But don't flatter yourself -- he's not sorry for you.
Zh: 两者都有一点。但是你可别太臭美——他可没为你感到抱歉。

En: Don't flatter yourself -- he's not going to miss you.
Zh: 你可别臭美——他不会想念你的。

En: Great! Any other complaints?
Zh: “很好！还有其他抱怨吗？”

En: I couldn't reach up and grab a tie from a spinning fan without unbearable pain shooting through my arm.
Zh: “我想伸手从一台旋转的电风扇上抓住一条领带，但是整条胳膊都疼的受不了。”

En: I've lost my memory. All of it.
Zh: “我失忆了。全部的记忆。”

En: Doc, someone broke my heart.
Zh: “医生，有人伤了我的心。”

En: Look, pal -- getting *pumped and dumped* is not a disease. People live through it all the time. You should be happy for her.
Zh: “听着，伙计——*热恋然后被甩*不是病。大家都是这么过来的。你应该为她感到高兴。”

En: Hard to tell exactly what it was over the phone. Could be a combination of peripheral neuralgia and high blood pressure. Could be that you were having a heart attack...
Zh: “通过电话很难看出是怎么回事。也许是外周神经痛加上高血压问题。可能是你的心脏病发作了……”

En: ...or, actually! He seems to be positively surprised by the idea. "It could be *both*, given your profile."
Zh: “……或者，其实呢！”他似乎对这个想法感到很惊讶。“根据你的情况，可能*两者*都有。”

En: Wait... both? But that sounds bad.
Zh: “等等……两者都有？听起来很糟糕啊。”

En: Yup.
Zh: “没错。”

En: So what do I do?
Zh: “那我该怎么办？”

En: Cut down on the drinking, pal. In fact, cut *off* the drinking. The drugs too. Anything else?
Zh: “少喝点酒吧，伙计。其实呢，最好是完全*戒酒*。药也是。还有其他事吗？”

En: Oh, but speed, sweet speed... It goes so well with alcohol, too...
Zh: 哦，但是兴奋剂，美妙的兴奋剂……跟酒精是那么的般配……

En: What's he going on about? You know that speed is great for you. But, shh, don't tell him -- he won't understand.
Zh: 他在瞎说什么呢？你知道兴奋剂对你很好。但是，嘘，别告诉他——他不会理解的。

En: I still *feel* pretty good though. I can probably take a lot of damage.
Zh: “不过我还是*感觉*挺好的。我可能承受了很大的伤害。”

En: Yes, positive thinking is a well-known cure-all. My suggestion is to exercise a lot.
Zh: “是的，积极思考是众所周知的万灵药。我的建议是多多锻炼。”

En: Which reminds me -- when are we finally going to get that speed?
Zh: 这就提醒我了——我们什么时候才能拿到兴奋剂？

En: That's because you're hungover -- even the radio waves coming from your direction smell like liquor. He pauses. "Do you feel like your eyes are twitching too?"
Zh: “那是因为你的宿醉——就算是通过无线电波，你那边闻起来都是一股酒味。”他停顿了一下。“你有没有觉得眼睛也有点抽搐？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: (Lie.) "No."
Zh: （撒谎。）“没有。”

En: That's the tiny vessels pulsating from high blood pressure, stupid officer. You might be sustaining permanent damage to your vision as we speak. Not much I can do about it. I'm not an *eye* doctor.
Zh: “那是高血压引起的小血管颤动，愚蠢的警官。就在我们说话的时候，你的视力可能都在持续遭受永久损伤。对此我就帮不上多大忙了。我不是*眼科*医生。”

En: But... what kind of doctor are you then?
Zh: “但是……你是什么医生啊？”

En: The busy kind. It's not life-threatening, just annoying. For you, that is, what with your reputation as a sharpshooter. Me -- I've got more important things to do.
Zh: “很忙的那种。没有生命危险，只是有点烦人。对于你来说，这就是你神枪手名声的附属品。至于我——还有更重要的事要做。”

En: The busy kind. It's not life-threatening, just annoying. For you. I've got more important things to do here.
Zh: “很忙的那种。没有生命危险，只是有点烦人。对于你来说。我这里还有更重要的事要做。”

En: Good. It would be an awful nuisance if your experiments with your blood pressure were to cause permanent damage to your vision. A nuisance to you, that is, on account of you being so proud of your marksmanship.
Zh: “很好。如果你的血压实验对视力造成了永久损伤，那就真的麻烦大了。当然，是你的麻烦，毕竟你对自己的枪法那么得意。”

En: Good. It would be an awful nuisance if your experiments with your blood pressure were to cause permanent damage to your vision. A nuisance to you, that is.
Zh: “很好。如果你的血压实验对视力造成了永久损伤，那就真的麻烦大了。当然，是你的麻烦。”

En: And you survived it! Congratulations. Are you mobile?
Zh: “你挺过来了！恭喜。你能动了吗？”

En: Yes...
Even better! Anything else? I wouldn't worry about that. Officers your age have coronary trouble all the time. Also -- death is a natural part of life.
Zh: “那就更好了！还有别的事吗？我倒是不担心那个。像你这种年纪的警官总是有冠状动脉的问题。而且——死亡不过是生命最自然的一部分。”

En: With all the damage you've been dealing yourself with drugs and alcohol, I'm not surprised.
Zh: “想想你用药物和酒精对自己造成的那些伤害，我一点都不觉得奇怪。”

En: What else? I'm not a *brain* doctor. Look on the bright side -- you've got a whole new life now. Use it wisely.
Zh: “还有什么吗？我不是*脑科*医生。往好的方面想——你现在拥有全新的生活了。明智一点使用吧。”

En: Isn't there *anything* you can do for me?
Zh: “你就不能为我*做点什么*吗？”

En: What, you want me to do blood work for you again, tell you just how bad things really are *across the board*? You want another rundown of everything collapsing inside your body?
Zh: “什么，你又想让我给你做血液检查，然后告诉你情况到底有多*糟糕*？你想让我把你身体里面所有坏掉的地方列出一个完整的清单吗？”

En: Yes. I want the truth!
Zh: “是的，我想知道真相！”

En: No, that doesn't sound like something I can handle right now...
Zh: “不了，听起来我目前还承受不了这种事情……”

En: I see your point. Might as well just get back to work.
Zh: “我明白你的意思了。我还是继续回去工作吧。”

En: Do that. I need to go. Some idiot has glued his eyelids shut with Cyanoacrylate. It *looks* like Mack Torson, but it's hard to say because his eyes are swollen...
Zh: “那就这样吧。我得走了。有个白痴用氰基丙烯酸盐把自己的眼皮黏在一起了。*看*起来像是麦克·托森，不过很难说，因为他的眼睛都肿了……”

En: And then that motherfucker Gottlieb -- reeking of schnapps -- ordered me into his butcher's room... Officer Fischer is in a storytelling mood -- he continues: "And I ask him, 'You sure, doc?' and he's cool as can be, 'Yeah, if you're in pain, we've gotta get that baby out...'"
Zh: “然后就是那个该死的戈特利布——臭烘烘的杜松子酒——命令我到他的屠宰室去……”费舍尔警官现在很想讲故事——他继续着：“然后我问他，‘你确定吗，医生？’，然后他就装的酷酷的，‘是啊，如果你觉得很疼，那我们就把那个宝贝弄出来……”

En: ...and so he removed my appendix *on the spot*, while the party was still on in the other room. And I was delirious with pain and all the schnapps I'd had, but I remember thinking -- man, that Gottlieb can stitch...
Zh: “……然后他*当场*就把我的阑尾割了下来，其他房间当时还在开派对呢。我喝了那么多杜松子酒，还疼得都快晕过去了，但是我还记得自己在想——天啦，戈特利布可真会缝的……”

En: Only a month later, when I got a chance to properly thank him, did I find out that *he* had no recollection of the event -- that's Gottlieb for ya. In league with some devil, he is, I'm sure of it. Cheers! The clinking of beer glasses is drowned in group conversation...
Zh: “过了一个月之后，我找到机会想感谢他，然后发现*他*压根都想不起那件事了——这就是戈特利布。跟恶魔勾结在一起，他绝对有，我很肯定。干杯！”啤酒杯碰撞的叮当声淹没在大家的对话声中……

En: Yes, it's Firewalker here.
Zh: “是的，我是火行者。”

En: Firewalker? Yes, yes you are. Just don't breathe in the general direction of your fire-feet. Actually, wait...
Zh: “火行者？是啊，你确实是。只要别对着你的火脚吸气。其实吧，等一下……”

En: ...do exactly that. Put yourself out of your misery. Take a deep diaphragmatic breath in and... You hear an exaggerated inhale and a long exhale on the other end of the line.
Zh: “……那就这样做吧。让自己远离痛苦。深深地吸进横膈膜里，然后……”你听见电话那头传来一阵夸张的吸气声，然后是一阵长长的呼气声。

En: The firewalker cannot die.
Zh: 火行者是不可以死的。

En: Self-immolation has been all the rage in Lo Manthang for a while now, especially among university students. It is particularly popular in Co Hoi, where such acts get the most press.
Zh: 自焚在罗马丹流行了一阵子，特别是在大学生之间。尤其是科霍伊，这种行为在那里报道的最多。

En: But why?
Zh: 但是为什么呢？

En: Young religious people protesting the Seraise government, which they consider to be oppressive. It's also got something to do with the encroaching Occidental influences on the local way of life. You have to *not* like a lot of things to set yourself on fire.
Zh: 年轻的宗教人士反对萨弗里政府，他们觉得受到了压迫。这也跟奥西登逐渐影响当地的生活方式有关。只有*不喜欢*生活中很多东西的时候，才会选择自焚吧。

En: A glorious sight: your body, tired from the vastness of the visions that haunt it,  burning up in a flame fed by your own living breath.
Zh: 一个状绝的景象：你的身体，为萦绕周围那巨大的幻象而感到疲累，在自己鲜活气息的供养下，在大火中熊熊燃烧。

En: This has been my plan all along. I want to protest the Coalition government.
Zh: “这一直是我的计划。我想反抗联盟政府。”

En: The smell of my own flesh cooking... I wonder what it would be like...
Zh: “我自己血肉烧焦的味道……不知道是什么样的……”

En: I feel like you're making fun of me.
Zh: “我觉得你在取笑我。”

En: 'Cop Sets Fire to Himself.' That would be quite an interesting conceptual piece, don't you think?
Zh: “‘点燃自己的警察。’从概念上讲好像挺有意思的，不是吗？”

En: Sounds more like a newspaper headline that will surprise absolutely no one. Now, do you have any *current, pressing* medical problems?
Zh: “在我听起来，更像是不会让任何人感到惊讶的新闻头条。现在，你还有什么*眼下，很迫切*的医学问题吗？”

En: From what I can tell, you're just playing into their hands -- disabling yourself from doing any actually useful work. Now, do you have any *current, pressing* medical problems?
Zh: “据我所知，你只不过是正中他们下怀——让自己失去了做任何有实际意义工作的能力。现在，你还有什么*眼下，很迫切*的医学问题吗？”

En: Please wonder on your own time. Now, do you have any *current, pressing* medical problems?
Zh: “你自己找时间想去吧。现在，你还有什么*眼下，很迫切*的医学问题吗？”

En: I'm the most serious person at this station, detective. Now, do you have any *current, pressing* medical problems?
Zh: “我是整个警局最严肃的人了，警探。现在，你还有什么*眼下，很迫切*的医学问题吗？”

En: It would be an artistic statement of some kind or another.
Zh: 这会是某种艺术性陈述，诸如此类的。

En: Young people are stupid.
Zh: 年轻人都很愚蠢。

En: I wish I was young and on fire.
Zh: 我希望自己很年轻，而且充满激情。

En: They should be forced to serve in the military.
Zh: 就应该强迫他们去服兵役。

En: That's awful. I don't want to think about it anymore. [Ignore thought.]
Zh: 真是太可怕了。我不想再想了。[忽略思维。]

En: Yes, especially young people in young countries. Anyway, answer the man.
Zh: 是的，特别是年轻国家的年轻人。不管怎么说，回答那个男人。

En: You can still be. There's time. Anyway, answer the man.
Zh: 你还有机会的。还有时间。不管怎么说，回答那个男人。

En: Uhm... they already are. Anyway, answer the man.
Zh: 呃……他们已经这么做了。不管怎么说，回答那个男人。

En: So you see it too? That my body is to become the last torch in the darkness?
Zh: “所以你也看见了？我的身体变成黑暗中最后的火炬？”

En: I see third-degree burns and a referral to a plastic surgeon whom you won't be able to afford. But that's in the future. Do you have any *current, pressing* medical problems?
Zh: “我看见三度烧伤，还有需要推荐给整形医生——当然你是负担不起的。不过那都是未来的事了。你还有什么*眼下，很迫切*的医学问题吗？”

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK
En: Assign it to someone else, Alice -- we've got enough on our plate.

En: It looked like a simple accident to me, Alice. No field autopsy necessary.

En: No.

En: You just made it up? Silence. "I see."

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK
En: You can hear her quickly typing in the background.

En: He was wearing boots, trousers and an old leather jacket with a bright blue lining. I found a library card from his pockets.

En: Any information on the library card?

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK
En: It's from Central Jamrock Public Library. It belongs to someone named Billie Méjean.

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK
En: I haven't examined it yet.

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK
En: You can hear her sigh audibly on the other side.

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK
En: So unprofessional of you... Pity!

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK

En: That's all for now. Thank you for reporting in. Is there anything else I can do for you?

ConvTitle: KINEEMA / REPORTING THE CORPSE ON THE BOARDWALK

Zh: “现在先到这里吧。谢谢你的汇报。还有什么能帮你的吗？”

Zh: “这是药剂师军团特殊订单的一部分，那是一家安保公司，负责保护奥兰治制药公司的利益——在塞美尼冲突发生期间。”

En: Sometimes police work is about human dignity -- about giving back names to anonymous victims.

En: No, and I doubt they'll get back to me before tomorrow morning. Anything else?

En: Sure, officer. What's the number? And the make of the armour, if you know it?

En: E50.100.1000. The make and model of the armour is Fairweather T-500 / VE.
Zh: “E50.100.1000。盔甲的制造商和型号是费尔韦瑟T-500/VE。”

En: X54156745678222. The make and model of the armour is Fairweather T-500 / VE.
Zh: “X54156745678222。盔甲的制造商和型号是费尔韦瑟T-500/VE。”

En: Are you sure that's the right number? Doesn't sound like a serial.
Zh: “你确定号码没错吗？听起来不太像序列号。”

En: That's definitely the right number.
Zh: “绝对就是这个号码。”

En: You're right. I meant to say E50.100.1000. Sorry for the confusion.
Zh: “你说的对。我本来想说E50.100.1000。抱歉，让你困惑了。”

En: Got it. I'll contact the ICP database immediately. Call back tomorrow. Hopefully they'll have dug up something useful by then.
Zh: “收到。我马上跟ICP资料库联系。明天再联系你。希望他们能找到什么有用的信息。”

En: There's a slightly weary, condescending note in her voice. She doesn't trust your professionalism following your rather tasteless come-ons.
Zh: 她的声音有一丝疲倦，还有些居高临下的意味。体验过你那些乏味的诱饵之后，她完全不相信你的专业程度。

En: There's just a hint of weariness in her voice. It's not that she doesn't trust you -- she just wants to be thorough so as not to create extra work for both of you.
Zh: 她的声音里有一丝疲倦的味道。不是说她不相信你——她只是想彻底一点，这样你们俩都不用额外做功了。

En: Well, I'll contact the ICP database immediately. Call back tomorrow. Hopefully they'll have dug up something useful to your investigation by then.
Zh: “好吧，我马上跟ICP资料库联系。明天再联系你。希望他们能找到对你的调查有帮助的信息。”

En: No problem, officer. Just glad we got it straightened out. I'll contact the ICP database immediately. Call back tomorrow. Hopefully they'll have dug up something useful to your investigation by then.
Zh: “没问题，警官。很高兴能澄清这一点。我马上跟ICP资料库联系。明天再联系你。希望他们能找到对你的调查有帮助的信息。”

En: AO5577789RHG9999. The make and model of the armour is Fairweather T-500 / VE.
Zh: “AO5577789RHG9999。盔甲的制造商和型号是费尔韦瑟T-500/VE。”

En: Wait, what's ICP?
Zh: “等一下，ICP是什么？”

En: The International Collaboration Police? Uhm... She seems confused for a second. "They keep detailed records of all military technology produced by licensed manufacturers. That's one of the reasons the Moralintern spearheaded the creation of the ICP in the first place."
Zh: “国际联合刑警？嗯……”她好像有点困惑。“所有特许生产商的军事技术产品，他们那里都有详细的记录。这也是国际道德伦理委员会一开始创建ICP的原因之一。”

En: Cool... and what's the Moralintern?
Zh: “厉害……还有，国际道德伦理委员会是什么？”

En: Officer, I suspect you're just trying to keep me on the line, and I just... don't have time for this. Do you have any other *serious* questions?
Zh: “警官，我怀疑你只是不想让我挂断吧，我……真的没有这个时间。你还有其他*严肃*的问题吗？”

En: Officer, as much as I appreciate your efforts to lighten the mood, we both have important work to get back to. Did you have any other *serious* questions?
Zh: “警官，我很感激你为舒缓情绪所做的努力，但是我们都有很重要的工作。你还有其他*紧急*问题吗？”

En: The International Collaboration Police (ICP) is an interisolary law enforcement service, the crown jewel in the Moralintern diadem alongside EPIS and the Coalition Government.
Zh: 国际联合刑警(ICP)是一个国际执法机构，跟EPIS和联盟政府一样，都是国际道德伦理委员会王冠上的宝珠。

En: They couldn't find anything on the full code you gave me. However, the first ten characters tracked to a custom-made baby stroller made in Vaasa City ten years ago.
Zh: “他们没能在你给我的完整代码上找到任何信息。不过，前10个字符追踪到十年前瓦萨城制造的一台定制婴儿車。”

En: They couldn't find anything on the full code you gave me. However, the first eight characters were match for a series of paintings by Tobias Pren -- you know, that artist who recently hanged himself?
Zh: “他们没能在你给我的完整代码上找到任何信息。不过，前8个字符跟托拜厄斯·普雷恩的一系列画作是相匹配的——你知道的，就是最近刚上吊自杀的那个艺术家？”

En: Yes. The armour was produced by... She looks at her notes. "Fairweather in their facilities in Betancourt, Sur-la-Clef, in '42."
Zh: “是的。这套盔甲的生产商是……”她看了一眼笔记。“费尔韦瑟在贝当古的一个加工点，就在苏拉菲，42年产的。”

En: She browses pages. "So... it seems the armour went to Semenine. That's where the paper trail ends, though. Even the firm has proven difficult to track. Corps de Pharmacie has been renamed several times over in the years since the armour was issued."
Zh: 她浏览着页面。“所以……看起来这套盔甲被送去了塞美尼。不过，书面记录到这里就中断了。就连公司也很难追踪。这套盔甲上市这几年来，药剂师军团连着改名好多次。

En: Does that seem relevant to your investigation?
Zh: “这跟你的调查有关系吗？”

En: No, but thank you for looking into it.
Zh: “没有，不过还是谢谢你帮我调查。”

En: Yes, absolutely! This is exactly what I wanted.
Zh: “有，当然有了！我需要的就是这个。”

En: Just doing my job, officer. Was there anything else?
Zh: “只是完成我的本职工作罢了，警官。还有什么事吗？”

En: Great. Anything else I could do for you?
Zh: “太好了。还有什么能帮到你的吗？”

En: This doesn't seem relevant to my investigation. (Conclude the conversation.)
Zh: “看起来好像跟我的调查没有关系。”（结束对话。）

En: Art is extremely relevant to my investigation. Thank you. (Conclude the conversation.)
Zh: “艺术跟我的调查密切相关。谢谢你。”（结束对话。）

En: What were his paintings like?
Zh: “他的画是什么样的？”

En: No, what were his paintings like?
Zh: “不知道，他的画是什么样的？”

En: Why did he hang himself?
Zh: “他为什么要上吊？”

En: No, why did he hang himself?
Zh: “不，他为什么要上吊？”

En: I'm not sure how this relates to my investigation yet, but I'm sure it does. (Conclude the conversation.)
Zh: “我不确定这跟我的调查有什么样的关系，不过我肯定是有的。”（结束对话。）

En: Very pretty pictures of assorted wildlife meant to inspire viewers and look good over couches in office building lobbies. She hesitates. "I've always sort of thought they were trash."
Zh: “各种各样漂亮的野生动物画，应该是打算放在办公大楼的大堂里激发观众的灵感，还有放在沙发上好看的吧。”她犹豫了一下。“我一直觉得那些都是垃圾。”

En: Celebrity problems.
Zh: “名声问题。”

En: She means pills and alcohol. See, pills and alcohol make you *a celebrity*. Hasn't it been a while since you've had any?
Zh: 她指的是药品和酒精。看见了吗，药品和酒精能让你变成一个*名人*。你有多久没碰过了？

En: It's all I could learn, I'm afraid. That's the serial number you gave me. Anything else I can do for you?
Zh: “恐怕我只知道这么多了——你给我的那个序列号。还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: I hope so. Anything else I can do for you?
Zh: “希望如此。还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Great. Anything else I can do for you?
Zh: “太好了。还有什么能帮到你的吗？”

En: I'm guessing one of its names is Krenel.
Zh: “我猜其中一个名字是科奈尔。”

En: Yes! She seems glad you guessed it. "That is the most recent name the ICP has been able to connect to the CdP. And the one before it was Downwell. I also have one called Sediment -- but I think that might be a different contractor..."
Zh: “是的！”她好像很开心你猜对了。“这是ICP联系到CdP最近的名字。在那个之前是深井。我这里还有一个叫赛迪蒙特的——不过我觉得可能是不同的佣兵组织……”

En: Do you know what it's called now?
Zh: “你知道它现在叫什么吗？”

En: The most recently registered firm that the ICP has been able to connect to the CdP is a military contractor called *Krenel*. And the one before it was Downwell. I think they might be the same contractor.
Zh: “ICP联系到CdP之后，找到最近注册的是一个名叫*科奈尔*的军事佣兵组织。在那个之前是深井。我想也许是同一个佣兵组织。”

En: A suit of armour like this would have been manufactured with a particular person's physique in mind. You should ask for whom this suit was fitted.
Zh: 像这样的一套盔甲，应该是按照某个人的体型专门制造的。你应该问问这套盔甲是给谁穿的。

En: First, has the firm continued to work for pharmaceutical companies through all these name changes?
Zh: “首先，这家公司改名这么多次之后，还在为制药公司工作吗？”

En: Yes, please do try. It's imperative that we learn whose armour this was.
Zh: “是的，请务必试试。我们必须知道这是谁的盔甲。”

En: Armour like this isn't mass-produced -- it would have probably been fitted. Perhaps there's a record of who signed for this particular suit of armour.
Zh: “这种盔甲一定不是批量生产的——很可能是量身定制。到底是谁签收过这套特殊的盔甲，也许还留有记录。”

En: A suit of armour like this would have been customized to fit the wearer. There must be a record of the person to whom it was issued.
Zh: “这种盔甲一定是根据穿戴者的体型量身定制的。应该还有签发记录吧。”

En: Is there any way to find out who got this particular suit of armour?
Zh: “有没有办法找到这套特殊盔甲的主人是谁？”

En: Yes, but the ICP tends to be reluctant to share private sector records. I could try to talk them into it, though.
Zh: “是的，但是ICP好像不愿意分享私人记录。不过我可以试试说服他们。”

En: Hard to say. Their client list is rather diverse. And... incomplete. The only constant seems to be that the mercenaries are always deployed in third- and fourth-world countries.
Zh: “很难说。他们的客户名单上形形色色的人都有。而且……很不完整。唯一不变的好像只有那些部署在第三和第四国家的雇佣兵。”

En: Sure. Call back tomorrow. Hopefully I will have more information for you then.
Zh: “当然。明天给你回话。希望到那时我能收到更多信息。”

En: This is a fun challenge for her, an opportunity to contribute beyond doing her job by rote. She'll gladly put in the extra effort for team RCM!
Zh: 对于她来说，这是个很有趣的挑战。除了那些呆板的本职工作外，她还能做出额外的贡献。她会很愿意为RCM警队付出额外的努力！

En: Yes, thank you, officer. I really appreciate your efforts in this case.
Zh: “是的，谢谢你，警官。很感谢你对案件做出的努力。”

En: You are my beautiful conquering heroine.
Zh: “你是我美丽的女英雄。”

En: Glad to help. Call back tomorrow. Hopefully I'll have more for you then.
Zh: “乐意帮忙。明天回复你。希望到那时我能收到更多消息。”

En: I'm going to ignore that, officer. Call back tomorrow. Hopefully I will have more information for you then.
Zh: “这个我就不管了，警官。明天回复你。希望到那时我能收到更多消息。”

En: Yes. I got the mercenary's name and a few biographical details. Are you ready?
Zh: “是的。我拿到了雇佣兵的名字，还有一些履历详情。你准备好了吗？”

En: Yes. It took some convincing, but I got the mercenary's name and a few biographical details. Are you ready?
Zh: “是的。确实需要些说服力，不过我拿到了雇佣兵的名字，还有一些履历详情。你准备好了吗？”

En: Shoot.
Zh: “说吧。”

En: I was born ready, baby!
Zh: “我生来就准备好了，宝贝！”

En: That suit of armour was issued to an Oranjese citizen named Ellis Kortenaer -- that's E-L-L-I-S K-O-R-T-E-N-A-E-R. Exact date of birth unknown. He was signed into the Lelystad County Neonatal Care Unit on 28th of February, ’09.
Zh: “那套盔甲签发给了奥兰治的一个市民，名叫埃利斯·科顿艾尔——拼写是E-L-L-I-SK-O-R-T-E-N-A-E-R。具体的出生日期不祥。09年2月28号，他被送到莱利斯塔德郡的新生儿加护病房。”

En: This is what the ICP knows about him: He was raised by foster parents, entered the Ijsbrand Military Academy -- in Vredefort -- at 17, then served in the Oranjese forces till he was honourably discharged in '41, just a year before the Semenese conflict.
Zh: “ICP那里只有这些信息：他被寄养家庭养大，17岁那年加入弗里德堡的艾斯布兰德军事学院，然后进入奥兰治部队服役，直到41年光荣退伍，就在塞美尼冲突发生的一年之前。”

En: Lelystad is probably where he got the name Lely.
Zh: “莱利斯塔德，大概他就是因此得名莱利的吧。”

En: Lelystad is probably where he got the name *Lely*.
Zh: “莱利斯塔德，大概他就是因此得名*莱利*的吧。”

En: I'm glad the inquiry was helpful to your investigation, officer. Did you have any other questions?
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你的调查，警官。你还有其他问题吗？”

En: A name -- this is very good. Ellis Kortenaer... he says to himself.
Zh: “一个名字——真是太好了。埃利斯·科顿艾尔……”他自言自语到。

En: Joyce said the dead man went by Lely. He must have gotten that from *Lelystad* -- the name of the county.
Zh: 乔斯说那个死者叫莱利。他一定是从*莱利斯塔德*得名的——那个郡名。

En: According to Klaasje, Lely said his real name wasn't really *his* -- perhaps that's because he was fostered?
Zh: 根据卡拉洁说的，莱利说他的真名其实不属于*他*——也许因为他是被寄养的？

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Was there anything else?
Zh: “还有其他事吗？”

En: I don't know about you, but I really, really enjoyed it. Worked out really well in the end.
Zh: 我不知道你怎么想的，但我真的，真的很喜欢。最后结果也挺不错的。

En: I knew this would lead to something good!
Zh: 吾辈就知道会有好结果的！

En: We're still glad you did it, my liege. This was good training. For when you need to lie to save your life maybe?
Zh: 吾辈为你感到很高兴，大人。这是一次很好的训练。也许您什么时候需要通过撒谎来挽救自己的性命？

En: You hear her fingers on a keyboard. "No, they were called *Sediment*. And *Somatosensor*, it seems. All that during the last nine years."
Zh: 你听见她的手指敲击键盘的声音。“不，他们被称为*赛迪蒙特*。好像还有*萨玛托塞斯*。这一切都发生在过去的九年里。”

En: The world will end soon.
Zh: “世界很快就会毁灭。”

En: Okay. She's not worried. "Does that mean you're ready?"
Zh: “好吧。”她并不担心。“这是不是意味着你已经准备好了？”

En: Neonatal Care Unit?
Zh: “新生儿加护病房？”

En: He was found as a newborn, in a leaf compactor, near an abandoned farm. He spent four months in the neonatal unit -- survived, apparently -- and was assigned to a foster family at two.
Zh: “他被发现的时候还是个婴儿，被放在一个废弃农场的树叶压缩机里。在新生儿加护病房待了4个月——显然是活下来了——然后2岁时被送到了一个寄养家庭。”

En:
Then the armour followed him to Semenine -- or at least I assume it did. And that's it. There are no records of his employment in Krenel. Or any of its other incarnations. Or him even entering Revachol.	“然后，盔甲跟着他去了塞美尼——或者，至少我假设如此。就这些。科奈尔没有他的工作记录。或者它的其他化身也没有。而且甚至没有他入境瑞瓦肖的记录。
0x0100007800012D43	Dialogue Text	KINEEMA / SERIAL NUMBER	Kim Kitsuragi	The lieutenant leans in to listen, notebook in hand.	警督手中拿着笔记本，倾身聆听着。
0x0100007800012D5B	Dialogue Text	KINEEMA / SERIAL NUMBER	You	So all we have to connect him to Krenel is the armour."
Zh: “所以他跟科奈尔的联系只有那套盔甲。”

En: Even that is a small miracle -- these *organizations* usually double check their inventory. He leans closer and shouts: "Thank you, Alice. Great work!"
Zh: “就连这都算是个小小的奇迹——这些*组织*一般都会仔细检查自己的存货。”他又靠近了一些，然后呼喊到：“谢谢你，爱丽丝。做得漂亮！”

En: No problem, lieutenant. She sounds pleased.
Zh: “不客气，警督。”她听起来很开心。

En: Even that took quite a bit of convincing to retrieve the information from the database, officer.
Zh: “即便如此，我还是花了不少功夫才说服他们从资料库中检索出这些信息的，警官。”

En: A compliment from Lieutenant Kitsuragi! Not bad.
Zh: 来自曷城警督的称赞！不赖嘛！

En: Wait, he was found in a... leaf compactor?
Zh: “等等，他是在……树叶压缩机里被发现的？”

En: It's a garden tool used to press leaves into these... cubes. It's a detail the hospital had -- the only detail in these files. So I thought it would be good for you to know.
Zh: “那是一种园艺工具，用来把树叶压缩成……方块的。这是医院记录的一条具体信息——这些文件里唯一的具体信息。所以我觉得，还是让你知道的好。”

En: It is! Thank you, Alice!
Zh: “没错！谢谢你，爱丽丝！”

En: Any information on his foster parents?
Zh: “关于他的寄养父母，有什么信息吗？”

En: None, officer. Sorry.
Zh: “没有，警官。抱歉。”

En: The lieutenant nods -- then jots it down in his notes: "Lelystad -- Lely. I like that."
Zh: 警督点点头——然后记在笔记里。“莱利斯塔德——莱利。我喜欢这个名字。”

En: If you say so, officer -- of course.
Zh: “如果你这么说的话，警官——当然了。”

En: So his name wasn't *his* because he was fostered?
Zh: “所以他的名字不属于*他自己*，是因为他是被寄养的？”

En: Hah! Yes -- what Klaasje said. Very good. That makes sense to me. He takes a quick note.
Zh: “哈！是的——卡拉洁就是这么说的。很好。我觉得很有道理。”他飞快地做下笔记。

En: I... don't know what that means. But -- sure.
Zh: “我……我不知道是什么意思。不过——肯定是的。”

En: Well, we have his name and service record now. (Move on.)
Zh: “呃，现在我们知道了他的名字和服役记录。”（继续。）

En: I'm glad the inquiry was helpful to your investigation, officer. Did you have any other questions?
Zh: “很高兴能帮到你的调查，警官。你还有其他问题吗？”

En: This means something to him. To know that name. Like naming a case. It's important.
Zh: 知道那个名字。对与他来说很重要。就像给案件起名一样，是很重要的。

En: 10-22 the captain?! This sounds bad -- bad *and* scary, like being called to the headmaster's office in school.
Zh: 10-22警长？！这听起来就有点糟糕了——*而且*还有点吓人，就好像是在学校被点名叫到校长办公室了。

En: Hit them with a comeback.
Zh: 反击他们。

En: 10-9, come in, officer! Did you get my question? We were wondering about your gun. Over.
Zh: “10-9，呼叫，警官！你听到我的问题了吗？我们想知道你的枪怎么样了。完毕。”

En: Don't sweat it, *bratan*. You don't need a gun to have fun... we can still have fun. It's not all over.
Zh: 别担心，*兄弟*。寻开心的话可用不着枪……没枪我们照样能开心。一切还没结束呢。

En: Lying over the phone? It's easy! Just say it like it's the truth -- and then it becomes it.
Zh: 在电话里撒谎？太简单了！就当是事实一样说出来——然后就会变成真的了。

En: Even before you can get the words out, everything gets scrambled in your brain.
Zh: 你的话还没说出来，脑子里就先乱成了一滩浆糊。

En: No, of course I didn't lose my gun.
Zh: “没有，我当然没有把枪搞丢啊。”

En: No, of course I didn't lose my fun. *Gun*. Fuck it! I didn't lose my gun!
Zh: “没有，我当然没有搞丢自己的“姜”！我是说*枪*。该死的！我怎么可能会把自己的枪搞丢！”

En: No, I think I'm good. I think I'm good without my badge, thanks.
Zh: “不，我觉得我没事。就算没了警徽，我也挺好的，谢谢。”

En: I'm going to report that you are in pursuit of your missing items. Over.
Zh: “我会回报说你在追查自己丢失的物品。完毕。”

En: Roger that. 10-10. Over and out. The static ends with a loud click, then everything is silent in the cabin.
Zh: “收到。10-10。通话完毕。”伴随着一身响亮的咔哒声，通话结束了。接着，乘坐舱里的一切都陷入了沉默。

En: 10-4, sir, I'm not hearing your question? the radio operator inquires again.
Zh: “10-4，先生，我没听到你的问题？”通讯员又询问了一次。

En: 10-4, I hear you. I don't have the authority to grant your request, but....
Zh: “10-4，我听见了。我无权答应你的请求，不过……”

En: What does he want now?
Zh: “他现在又想干什么？”

En: I'm happy to report that I have found my badge, Jules.
Zh: “我很高兴地告诉你：我找到警徽了，朱尔斯。”

En: 10-4, sir, glad to hear that! I'll write down that there's no need to issue a new one to you then. Over.
Zh: “10-4，先生，很高兴听到这个消息！我会记下来，不用给你派发新的了。完毕。”

En: Who cares what this Vicquemare said -- pull on their heart strings!
Zh: 谁在乎维克玛说了什么——牵动他们的心弦！

En: Enough, officer. This begging is below your dignity.
Zh: 够了，警官。乞求有失你的自尊。

En: What? I thought you had your shit together! This is nearing a complete meltdown! Stop it!
Zh: 什么？我还以为你他妈没事呢！这他妈差一点就要完全崩溃了！快停下！

En: Hold on, are you alone in the room? I need some confidential information about myself.
Zh: “等一下，你一个人在房间里吗？我需要一些自己的机密信息。”

En: That's a negative, sir, I got a 10-12 here. Over.
Zh: “答案是否定的，先生，10-12也在这里。完毕。”

En: That's a negative, sir, I got a 10-12 (Visitors Present) here. Over.
Zh: “答案是否定的，先生，10-12（访客在场）也在这里。完毕。”

En: Please refer to me with my last name in the future.
Zh: “以后请用姓氏称呼我。”

En: Stop calling me *sir* and just use my real god damn name, will you?!
Zh: “别再叫我*先生*了，用我的真名，好吗？！”

En: Uh...
Some *talk*? What does it even mean there's been some talk? Do they think I'm corrupt or not?!
Zh: “有些*传言*？有传言到底是什么意思？他们到底觉得我是受贿了还是没有？！”

En: Does it mean people know that I'm a dirty cop? Because I am. I'm the dirtiest, most dangerous cop in Revachol. My reputation precedes me.
Zh: “那是不是说大家都知道我是个黑警？因为我就是。我是整个瑞瓦肖城里最黑心，最危险的警察。我有些名过其实了。”

En: I want you to know that I would *never ever* do anything like that. I'm not taking bribes, Jules!
Zh: “我想让你知道，我*再也不会*做出那种事了。我没有收受贿赂，朱尔斯！”

En: I hope you're joking, sir. His voice is dry and humourless. "I only meant that there's been some talk in the station, that's all. But there's always some talk in the station. You know how officers in Jamrock are..."
Zh: “我希望你在开玩笑，先生。”他的声音干巴巴的，一点也不幽默。“我只是想说——警局里是有一些传言，就这样。不过警局总是有各种各样的传言。你也知道加姆洛克的警官什么样……”

En: But then again, some of us truly are on the take. It's unfortunate. Over.
Zh: “不过话又说回来，我们中确实有人受贿了。真的很遗憾。完毕。”

En: Do you need anything else? Over.
Zh: “你还有什么别的需要吗？完毕。”

En: Do you need anything else? Over.
Zh: “你还有什么别的需要吗？完毕。”

En: 10-4, I hear you, officer. I'm just going to make a note here that you are in pursuit of your *misplaced* badge.
Zh: “10-4，我听见了，警官。我这边会留下记录——你正在搜寻*丢失*的警徽。完毕。”

En: 10-22 the captain?! This sounds bad.
Zh: “10-22警长？！听起来不太妙啊。”

En: What about... He seems to be hesitating. "What about your gun, officer? Do you still have your gun, sir? Over."
Zh: “那……”他好像有点犹豫。“那你的枪呢，警官？你的枪还在吗，先生？完毕。”

En: 10-9, come in, officer! Did you get my question? I was wondering about your gun. Over.
Zh: “10-9，呼叫，警官！你听到我的问题了吗？我想知道你的枪怎么样了。完毕。”

En: Check your pockets! Check your... holy fuck, you really don't know where it is, do you?!
Zh: 检查一下你的口袋！检查一下你的……真他妈该死，你不知道它在哪，是吗？！

En: Okay, it's gone. Your gun is most definitely gone.
Zh: 好吧，没了。你的枪绝对是没了。

En: Chillax, partner. You don't need a gun to have fun... although it helps.
Zh: 放松点，搭档。不用枪，你也能找到乐子……虽然它是有点帮助。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: 10-1, you're breaking up. 10-9, repeat please. Over.
Zh: “10-1，你的信号断了。10-9，请重复。完毕。”

En: Don't say anything.
Zh: 什么也不要说。

En: Remain silent.
Zh: 保持沉默。

En: 10-9, come in, officer! Over.
Come in, officer! 10-9, repeat message please. Did you misplace your firearm? Over.
Zh: “呼叫，警官！10-9，请重复消息。你把枪忘在什么地方了吗？完毕。”

En: Just say you lost your gun. Anything else would only make the situation worse here.
Zh: 就说你把枪弄丢了。说别的只会让情况变得更糟糕。

En: Affirmative? There's a pause.
Zh: “肯定？”对方停顿了一下。

En: Quietly turn the radio off and put the microphone back to its holder.
Zh: 静静地把无线电关小，然后把麦克风放回到支架上。

En: That's affirmative. Over.
Zh: “那是肯定的。完毕。”

En: He didn't actually expect that answer.
Zh: 他没想到会得到这样的答案。

En: I'm going to report that you are in the process of recovering your service weapon. Over.
Zh: “我会汇报说你正在寻找自己的配枪。完毕。”

En: The man uses relay code. You got this. You're a cop and cops *know* relay code.
Zh: 这个人使用的是转播编码。你可以的。你是个警察，警察当然*懂得*转播编码。

En: DEFEND YOURSELF. Immediately. They're laughing at you.
Zh: 为自己辩护。马上！他们在嘲笑你。

En: Mullen dicked us...
Zh: “马伦害惨我们了……”

En: Oh god, it's not here...
Zh: 天啦，不在这里……

En: I DON'T HAVE MY GUN?!
Zh: 我的枪没了？！

En: No, it's gone. It's not fucking *on* you!
Zh: 没，它不见了。它真他妈的不在你*身上*！

En: (Yell.) "Right fucking here! I have my Villiers right here! I found it!"
Zh: （大喊）“他妈的就在这里！我的维利耶就在这里！我找到它了！”

En: A sigh of relief crackles over the radio. "10-4. He says he's located it."
Zh: 无线电那头传来一阵如释重负的叹息声。“10-4。他说找到了。”

En: Okay, I lied. I don't have it.
Zh: “好吧，我撒谎了。不在我身上。”

En: Officer, do you need further assistance? Over.
Zh: “警官，你需要进一步的协助吗？完毕。”

En: Oh god, I... The man is fighting back tears.
Zh: “天啦，我……”男人强忍住泪水。

En: In pursuit of his... The man is fighting back tears.
Zh: “在搜寻他的……”男人强忍住泪水。

En: In pursuit of his... The man is fighting back tears.
Zh: “在搜寻他的……”男人强忍住泪水。

En: Nothing is working!
Zh: 什么都没用！

En: That's it. He pulled the plug.
Zh: 就是这个了。他拔掉插头。

En: Sir, I will not have you talk to me in this manner. Over.
Zh: “先生，我不允许你这样对我说话。完毕。”

En: SAY MY NAME BITCH! What are you doing, you sound way too aggressive.
Zh: 叫我的名字啊，贱人！你在干什么，你的话实在是太咄咄逼人了。

En: You're blowing it.
Zh: 你搞砸了。

En: Oldboy,  is fucky *still* at it?! What the hell does he want now?
Zh: “老小子，那个混账玩意*还在*线上吗？！他现在又想干什么？”

En: Tell him to sober up and blow himself.
Zh: “叫他清醒一点，自己解决下。”

En: *What* the fuck does he want now? More attention? A fiver? A parade?
Zh: “马伦现在又想要*什么*？更多关注？钱？炫耀？”

En: No, I think he's requesting personal details of his life. He returns to you. "Officer, you've never shared things of that nature with me. Over."
Zh: “不，我觉得他想要自己的个人信息。”他转回你这边。“警官，你没有跟我说过这种信息。完毕。”

En: What happened is my partner made contact -- it's not good. He's lost his badge *and* his sidearm. He seemed confused, delirious even... He stops to think.
Zh: “现在的情况是：我的搭档主动联系我——这不是什么好事。他弄丢了自己的警徽，*还有*配枪。他好像很困惑，甚至有点神志不清……”他停下来想了想。

En: What happened is my partner made contact. It's not good. He seemed confused, delirious even... Vicquemare stops to think.
Zh: “现在的情况是：我的搭档主动联系我。这不是什么好事。他好像很困惑，甚至有点神志不清……”维克玛停下来想了想。

En: What happened is my partner made contact. And it's not good. He's lost his badge. He seemed confused, delirious even... He stops to reflect.
Zh: “现在的情况是我的搭档主动联系我。这不是什么好事。他弄丢了自己的警徽。他好像很困惑，甚至有点神志不清……”他停下来沉思着。

En: Far north -- on the other side of the motorway -- the officer quietly hunches with his hand in the motor carriage.
Zh: 北边稍远的地方——在高速公路的另一侧——警官沉默地弯下腰，把手伸进机車里。

En: Good, Vicquemare turns to the others, "Okay everybody, nothing but a prank call here. We all got our laughs, now get back to work!"
Zh: “很好，”维克玛转向其他人：“好了，大家，只是个恶作剧电话。我们也开心够了，现在继续回去工作吧！”

En: It's not good.
Zh: “不是太好。”

En: Feels good to get that off your chest, right? Celebration with beer?
Zh: 把这事说出来感觉很好，对吗？来杯啤酒庆祝一下？

En: Uhm... you don't have a comeback, sorry. It's hard to think like this.
Zh: 嗯……你没有反败为胜，抱歉。很难这样想。

En: Uhm...
Oh fuck...
Zh: 该死……

En: For starters, it's *massive*. Got flared cooling vents along the front and hydrogen flasks sticking out too.
Zh: “对于新手来说，它很*厚重*。前面有喇叭形的冷却口，而且氢气瓶也突出来了。”

En: Dick Mullen is *not* your name. It's the name of a *fictional* detective who would *not* lose his badge.
Zh: 你的名字*不是*迪克·马伦。这是小说里一个*虚构*警探的名字，而且他*不会*弄丢自己的警徽。

En: Now is a good time to say "fuck" and "ass" and so on, that'll make this all right.
Zh: 现在是最适合说些“卧槽”和“放屁”之类的话，这样就没事了。

En: I'm still packing my Villiers.
Zh: “我还在装填我的维利耶。”

En: A sigh of relief crackles over the radio. "10-4. Over."
Zh: 无线电里传出一阵如释重负的叹息。“10-4。完毕。”

En: A sigh of relief crackles over the radio. "10-4. He says he's got it."
Zh: 无线电里传出一阵如释重负的叹息。“10-4。他说在他身上。”

En: I have my Villiers right here.
Zh: “我的维利耶就在这里呢。”

En: Losing his badge is bad enough. Tell him to find it and fast! We can't have some gang banger running around with it.
Zh: “搞丢警徽已经够糟糕的了。告诉他赶紧把东西找回来！我们可不能让青少年罪犯带着它到处跑。”

En: Thank god for that! That would have been a nightmare. I don't even want to imagine the poor *shit* who has to relay that kind of news to the captain..
Zh: “谢天谢地！要不然绝对会是一个噩梦。我甚至都不敢想象，哪个*可怜虫*敢把这种消息汇报给警长……”

En: Another crackled sigh of relief. "Anything else we can do for you? Over."
Zh: 又是一阵如释重负的叹息。“还有什么能帮到你的吗？完毕。”

En: I'm doing my best, okay?
Zh: “我已经尽力了，好吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Don't be an asshole, I'm on the case.
Zh: “别犯浑了，我正在处理这个案子。”

En: 10-4, sir. Sorry, but I'm under orders to give a negative to requests for personal information. Over.
Zh: “10-4，先生。抱歉，但是我奉命不能透露任何个人信息。完毕。”

En: You mean you can't tell me *anything* about me? But I'm me!
Zh: “你的意思是说，你*什么*也不能告诉我了？但是，是我自己的信息哎！”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白。”

En: 10-4. Anything else, sir? Over.
Zh: “10-4。还有什么事吗，先生？完毕。”

En: 10-4. Orders are still orders. Anything else? Over.
Zh: “10-4。命令就是命令。还有什么事吗？完毕。”

En: The lieutenant's eyes go wide. "But you said..." He shakes his head. "Never mind."
Zh: 警督睁大眼镜。“但你不是说……”他摇摇头。“算了。”

En: We're all glad to hear you've not lost your gun, officer. Do you need further assistance? Over.
Zh: “听到你的警枪没有弄丢，我们大家都很高兴，警官。你需要进一步的援助吗？完毕。”

En: Didn't you glue your eyes shut earlier? And now you're making fun of *me*?!
Zh: “你之前不是用胶水把眼睛粘上了吗？现在又开始取笑*我*了？！”

En: The man is laughing too hard to form coherent sentences. The only words you can make out are 'lost' and 'badge'.
Zh: 那人笑得太厉害，一句连贯的话都说不出来。你唯一能辨认出来的词只有‘丢失’和‘警徽’。

En: I'm looking for my address. I don't know where I live.
Zh: “我正在找我的地址。我不知道自己住哪。”

En: 10-4. Your address---
Hold up, Jules. He doesn't fucking know where he lives?! Did I hear that right?
Zh: “等等，朱尔斯。他妈的，他不知道自己住哪？！我没听错吧？”

En: Seems like it, sir.
Zh: “好像是的，先生。”

En: No. Fuck him. Don't tell him anything, you understand? He's been hellbound for the gutter, let him have it.
Zh: “不。去他妈的。什么都不要告诉他，你明白了吗？他已经泡进地狱的阴沟里了，让他自作自受。”

En: Sorry, sir. Anything more I can do for you? Over.
Zh: “抱歉，先生。还有什么我能为你做的吗？完毕。”

En: 10-4. Your badge should have most of your personal details? Look over that. Over.
Zh: “10-4。你的警徽里应该包含了你的大部分个人信息？仔细检查一下吧。完毕。”

En: I, uh... I don't have it. My badge.
Zh: “我，呃……我没有。警徽。”

En: Of course. My badge. I can look at my badge that I have. (Lie.)
Zh: “当然了。我的警徽。我可以看看警徽。”（撒谎。）

En: 10-4. Anything else for you, sir? Over.
Zh: “10-4。还有别的事吗，先生？完毕。”

En: Sergeant Torson here is wondering if you are still in possession of your genitalia. Over.
Zh: “托森警长想知道你的命根子还在不在。完毕。”

En: Yes, I lost my wiener too, just lay off, okay?
Zh: “是的，我的大香肠也弄丢了，消停点，行吗？”

En: I left it at his momma's after I fucked her ass all night. Tell him that.
Zh: “每天晚上跟他老娘走完后门之后，我就把宝贝放在她那里了。你就跟他这么说。”

En: I don't need to listen to this. Firewalker out! (Turn off the radio.)
Zh: “我不需要听这个。火行者下线！”（关掉收音机。）

En: That did not go well. I guess the important thing is that you followed protocol and reported your lost items.
Zh: “看来不太顺利啊。我觉得你应该遵守规章制度，上报不见的东西，这很重要。”

En: Did we need anything else here? He looks around in the cabin.
Zh: “还有什么别的需要吗？”他四下看了看乘車舱。

En: The silence is almost deafening after you flick off the switch, severing the connection.
Zh: 在你关掉开关，断开连接后，那片寂静几乎有点震耳欲聋。

En: Thank god for that! That would have been a nightmare. I don't even want to imagine the poor prick who has to relay that kind of news to the captain.
Zh: “谢天谢地！要不然绝对会是一场噩梦。我甚至都不敢想象，哪个可怜虫敢把这种消息汇报给警长。”

En: This might sound odd, but there's *personal details* I'd like to discuss.
Zh: “也许听起来会有些奇怪，但我想谈谈*个人信息*。”

En: I guess I can hold off the report for a few days. Oldboy lights another cigarette.
Zh: 老男孩又点燃一支烟，然后说到：“我猜我可以把报告推迟几天。”

En: Uh, can you repeat that?
Zh: “啊，你能重复一遍吗？”

En: This shit does *not* leave this room! Not a word of this to the captain or anyone else. We'll give him a couple of days to pull his shit together!
Zh: “这件破事*不能*从这个房间传出去！这句话千万别跟警长或者其他任何人说。我们会给他几天时间，让他赶紧他妈的振作起来！”

En: The fuck do you need a gun for? Look at the pythons on your arms. You *are* a gun. The biggest one in the world.
Zh: 你他妈要枪干嘛？看看你胳膊上的蟒蛇。你*就是*一支枪，世界上最大的枪。

En: She sighs. "Right... please hold."
Zh: 她叹了口气。“好吧……请等一下。”

En: Just a moment, officer...
Zh: “稍等一下，警官……”

En: I'm not going to...
Zh: “我不会这么说的……”

En: Ask him!
Zh: “问他！”

En: Mack, man the door, he gestures Torson to block the doorway, then turns to Minot. "You know what he told me? 'I don't want to get better -- I want to get worse.' Those were his words." He sighs heavily and turns to address the room...
Zh: “麦克，守好门，”他朝托森打了个手势，让他堵住门口，然后转向迈诺特。“你知道他跟我说什么吗？‘我不想变好——我想变得更糟糕’这是他的原话。”他重重地叹了口气，然后转向房间……

En: I just know we can't give up on him when he's at his weakest. He wouldn't... The crowd in the room has started fidgeting uncomfortably. Someone's trying to slip out unnoticed.
Zh: “我只知道我们不能在他最脆弱的时候放弃他。他不会……”房间里的众人开始有些坐立不安。有人想乘人不注意偷溜出去。

En: Just a second, officer... She puts you on hold, the static crackling softly like a bonfire. After a while you hear an old man greet you from the radio. His rattly voice is oddly familiar:
Zh: “稍等一下，警官……”她把你放到保留状态，电流像营火一样，轻轻发出噼里啪啦的声响。过了一会儿，你听到一个电话那头传来一个老人的问候声，他那沙哑的声音让你觉得出奇的熟悉：

En: That's a negative. Not going to say that. Over.
Zh: “那就是否定了。我不会这么说的。完毕。”

En: What's he saying? Share with the class!
Zh: “他说了什么？跟我们分享一下？”

En: Yeah? How do you fucking plan to do that, huh?! Get him off the drink?! Go jogging with him in the morning and get him on carrot juice?! He's a lost man!
Zh: “是吗？！那你他妈的打算怎么搞，哈？！让他戒酒？！早晨跟他一起慢跑，让他喝胡萝卜果汁？！他已经彻底迷失了！”

En: He... He said he sodomized your mother.
Zh: “他……他说跟你母亲走过后门。”

En: We must help him. Minot looks down at her neatly polished black shoes. There is a quiet firmness to her voice when she speaks.
Zh: 迈诺特低头看着她那双锃亮的黑色皮鞋。当她说话时，她的声音平静而坚定：“我们必须帮他。”

En: The prick ate momma's vanilla waffles at the captain's birthday party! Some nerve he's got...
Zh: “那个混蛋在警长的生日宴会上吃过妈妈做的香草华夫饼！他真有胆量敢……”

En: Sure her vanilla waffles were the only thing he ate?
Zh: “确定他只吃了她的香草华夫饼吗？”

En: Shut up, Chester, this isn't funny. This is my mom we're talking about!
Zh: “闭嘴，切斯特，这一点也不好笑。你们说的可是我妈！”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Tell him to apologize right now!
Zh: “让他现在就给我道歉！”

En: 10-4, come in, officer! Over.
Sergeant Torson requests that you apologize for the claims you made about his mother. Over.
Zh: “托森警长要求你为刚才说出的关于他母亲的那些言论道歉。完毕。”

En: 10-4, come in... He hesitates. "...*Fire walker*. Over."
Zh: “10-4，呼叫……”他有些犹豫。“……*火行者*。完毕。”

En: Yeah, Mullen was fucked alright. Sounded fucking drunk to me.
Zh: 他突然插嘴进来：“是啊，马伦他妈的搞砸了。我听起来应该是他妈的喝醉了。”

En: Okay, tell him I'm sorry -- It was just banter, I thought that's what cops do.
Zh: “好吧，告诉他我很抱歉——开个玩笑而已，没有恶意，我觉得警察都会这么干。”

En: He says he's sorry and he didn't mean nothing by it.
Zh: “他说他很抱歉，还有他不是有意的。”

En: He what...? He lost his badge?!
Zh: “他什么……？他的警徽不见了？！”

En: Okay, tell him...
Zh: “好吧，告诉他……”

En: Enough! The commotion dies down. All eyes turn to Satellite-Officer Vicquemare. "None of this is funny! It's fucking sad, that's what it is. He's a cop. He's one of us, goddamn this..."
Zh: 随迁警官维克玛咬住指关节，接着一阵吼声传遍整个房间：“够了！”骚乱瞬间平息下来。所有目光都转向了他。“这一点都不好笑！真他妈太悲哀了，这他妈叫悲哀。他是个警察。他是我们中的一员，真他妈该死……”

En: Tell him to find his *goddamn* badge and gun, that's the *only* thing that matters here!
Zh: “告诉他，赶紧找到他那*该死*的警徽和警枪，这是眼下*唯一*重要的事！”

En: 10-2, 10-5. This is 41st, come in! Over.
I'm his *goddamn* partner!
Zh: “我是他*该死的*搭档！”

En: Yeah, Mack's right, that was some gnarly shit there...
Zh: 他右边那个高个子笑的眼泪还没擦干。“是啊，麦克说得对，妈的痴线……”

En: You *really* need to get your shit together, this is nearing a complete meltdown.
Zh: 你*真的*需要好好振作起来，现在离彻头彻尾的灾难已经很近了。

En: Fire. Walker.
Zh: “火。行者。”

En: Fire-*water*? He's lost it... Fuck it, tell him to find his goddamn badge and gun, that's the only thing that matters!
Zh: “火*什么*？他失心疯了……去他妈的，告诉他，赶紧找到他那该死的警徽和警枪，这是眼下唯一重要的事！”

En: Hey, if you don't like the fallout, maybe *don't* fuck with the Firewalker!
Zh: “喂，如果你不想自食恶果，那也许就*不要*惹毛火行者！”

En: Who?! The disbelief in Vicquemare's voice is overwhelming.
Zh: “谁？！”维克玛的声音里有几分难以置信的味道。

En: 10-2 (Receiving Well), 10-5 (Relay Message). This is 41st, come in! Over.
Zh: “10-2（接收良好），10-5（转达口信）。这里是41分局，呼叫！完毕。”

En: Wrap it up, don't indulge in his drunken antics!
Zh: “打住，别再沉溺在他酒后的丑态里了！”

En: Listen, I've actually lost my gun, too.
Zh: “听着，我其实还把枪搞丢了。”

En: Mack, he says maybe you shouldn't have antagonised the Firewalker in the first place.
Zh: “麦克，他说也许你不应该一开始惹恼火行者。”

En: Satellite-Officer Vicque---
Zh: “随迁警官维克玛……”

En: What do you need, sir? Over.
Zh: “你有什么需要，先生？完毕。”

En: Mack 'The Torso' Torson is finger-fucking his fist, laughing hoarsely and apparently telling some dirty story to his partner, Chester McLaine, near the entrance.
Zh: ‘躯干’·麦克·托森用手指头套弄着他的拳头，沙哑地大笑着，显然是在跟自己的搭档，入口处的切斯特·麦克莱恩讲着什么下流的故事。

En: I am afraid he might be referring to himself as *Firewater*, sir.
Zh: “恐怕他自称是*火行者*，先生。”

En: A scrawny old man sits in a dusty cubicle, smoking, with a large white rectangle sewn on his vest. In front of him is a box-shaped apparatus with an array of wires and antennas -- the radio switchboard.
Zh: 一个骨瘦如柴的老人站在一个满是灰尘的小隔间里抽着烟，马甲上还绣着一个巨大的白色长方形。在他面前是一个盒子形状的装置，上面布满一排排电线和天线——是无线电交换机。

En: He acknowledges your joke and asks you to lay off.
Zh: “他收到了你的笑话，要你消停点。”

En: Lay off? Lay off?! Tell him we'll lay off when he retrieves the goddamn police property that he has been entrusted with.
Zh: “消停？他说消停？！告诉他，等他把上面郑重托付给他的警方财物找回来之后，我们再消停。”

En: 10-4, I know, I already wrote it in a report, but... He hesitates. "It will stay on my desk for a few days. Over."
Zh: “10-4，我知道，我已经写进报告了，但是……”他有些犹豫。“它几天后才会出现在我的桌子上。完毕。”

En: Does he actually want something or is he hell-bent on disrupting our work?!
Zh: “他是真的想要干什么，还是不顾一切地想要干扰我们的工作？！”

En: Satellite-Officer...
Zh: “随迁警官……”

En: 10-9, repeat message! I didn't get that, sir. Over.
Zh: “10-9，重复消息！我没听明白，先生。完毕。”

En: No, I think I'm good. I think I'm good without my gun, thanks.
Zh: “不，我觉得我没事。就算没了枪，我也挺好的，谢谢。”

En: I heard him and I'm on it.
Zh: “我听到他的话了，马上就去办。”

En: You probably don't *want* to continue on this road.
Zh: 也许你不*想*继续这条路了。

En: 10-4, affirmative. Officer is in pursuit of his firearm. There's static.
Zh: “10-4，肯定。警官在追查他的武器。”有一点杂音。

En: I'm going to report that you are in pursuit of your service weapon. Over.
Zh: “我会回报说你在追查自己的警枪。完毕。”

En: 10-4, Station 41. I've got a urgent business. Over.
Zh: “10-4，41分局。我有急事。完毕。”

En: Hi, this is... *me* here, I work at your station.
Zh: “嗨，是……*我*，我在你们警局工作。”

En: 10-... uh... excuse me, sir? Over.
Zh: “10-……呃……请原谅，先生？完毕。”

En: The officer is describing his genitalia in exaggerated terms. Over.
Zh: “这名警官在用夸张的语言描述他的命根子。完毕。”

En: It's got a magnetic accelerator an *arc* that accelerates the energized ball of periwinkle-blue hydrogen-based *blaasma* to near light speed...
Zh: “它有一个电磁加速器，还有一个*电弧*，可以将氢基滨螺蓝*等离子*的能量球加速到接近光速……”

En: Click, your index finger goes as the thumb cocks the hammer, click, cock, click... and then nothing. Three-shot, probably. Needs reloading. Break it open.
Zh: 咔哒，你用食指和翘起的大拇指做成锤子的样子，咔哒，嘀嗒，咔哒……然后停下来。大概是敲了三下。需要重新填充。把它打开。

En: You are describing a Villiers three-shot 9mm Pepperbox with a revolving barrel.
Zh: 你在描述一支维利耶9毫米、带转筒的三发转管手枪。

En: So, was there anything else?
Zh: “所以，你还有别的事吗？”

En: You really think I'd lose my *Villiers 9mm*? Nine millimetres of red hot leaden pepper for you right here, boys!
Zh: “你真以为我搞丢*维利耶9毫米*手枪了？小伙子们，给你们看看，9毫米火红热辣的铅制小辣椒！”

En: Triangular barrel, three shot, hammer at the end -- what else do you want from me? To tell you all about my wooden handle? Lay off, boys.
Zh: “三角枪管，三发，尾部有锤子——你还想从我这里得到什么？跟你说说我的木柄吗？消停点吧，小伙子们。”

En: He's describing his Pepperbox alright.
Zh: “他在描述自己的转管手枪。”

En: 10-4 (Message Received), 10-5 (Relay Message). What's your status? Over.
Zh: “10-4（收到消息），10-5（转达消息）。请报告你现在的情况？完毕。”

En: 10-4, what's your status? Over.
Zh: “10-4，你现在什么情况？完毕。”

En: Well, I guess he'd better crack the case before sundown then!
Zh: “好吧，那我猜他最好在太阳下山前把案子给破了！”

En: 10-4, well that's a...
He isn't getting a red cent, you can tell him that!
Zh: “他一分钱也拿不到，你可以直接告诉他！”

En: (Speak into the radio.) "It's a single-shot Armistice Kiejl A9. Over."
Zh: （对着无线电说。）“是单发枪，基耶尔止战者A9。完毕。”

En: Says it's a Kiejl 9mm...  Armistice.
Zh: “说它是一把基耶尔A9……止战者。”

En: Armistice? What, is he a fucking...? Clearly he doesn't have his Villiers anymore.
Zh: “什么？止战者？他妈的，难道他……显然他的维利耶没了。”

En: What is going on here? Did something happen? she asks, startled. Jean Vicquemare turns to her and says...
Zh: “这是怎么回事？出什么事了吗？”她惊恐地询问到。让·维克玛转向她，然后说到……

En: Understood, sir. Over.
Zh: “明白，先生。完毕。”

En: In contact it *detonates* with the power of a dying star, erasing every living thing from existence. Oh, and did I mention it is dripping with sexy blue blaasma?
Zh: “与它接触时，它会*引爆*出一颗垂死之星的能量，消灭所有生命的存在。哦，我有没有提过它还能发射性感的蓝色等离子光束？”

En: Uh... I'm going to need to put you on speaker for a second. Over.
Zh: “呃……我要开一下扬声器。完毕。”

En: Just reporting in. Over.
Zh: “只是报道一下。完毕。”

En: My status? I, uh...
Zh: “我的情况？我，呃……”

En: Sounds like he's being deliberately vague.
Zh: “听起来他是故意含糊其辞。”

En: Detective Mullen has done it again! 'Uh... where's my gun?' the speaker crudely mimics your voice.
Zh: “马伦警探又来了！‘呃……我的枪呢？’”说话者粗鲁地模仿着你的声音。

En: Dear god, he lost his gun!!! Oh my... I can't... He... The man succumbs to laughter again.
Zh: “我的天啦，他的枪不见了！！！我的天……这简直……他怎么能……”那人忍不住又笑了起来。

En: Mack can face the Giant of Koko Nur by himself, but Disco here made him piss his pants!
Zh: “麦克可以自己单挑科科努尔巨人，但是迪斯科却让他尿裤子了！”

En: Oh I... I can't... Fuuuuuck, he lost his... Ask him if he still has his wiener!
Zh: “哦我……我……我不能……该死，他弄丢了他的……问问他的香肠还在不在！”

En: This isn't really a laughing matter...
Zh: “这真的不是闹着玩的……”

En: (Put the microphone back in its holder and quit the call.)
Zh: （把话筒放回支架，结束通话。）

En: That's affirmative. Over.
Zh: “那是肯定的。完毕。”

En: I just thought you might have heard of them, that's all.
Zh: “我只是觉得你可能听说过他们，仅此而已。”

En: He says he doesn't have it.
Zh: “他说他没有。”

En: Anything else, sir? Over.
Zh: “还有别的事吗，先生？完毕。”

En: Nothing. Never mind.
Zh: “没什么。没事了。”

En: Say. My. Name.
Zh: “说出。我的。名字。”

En: It's a gun, what can I say? A regular god damn murder weapon, you know how they are -- *bang-bang*.
Zh: “它可是枪，我能说什么？一把普通的该死的杀人武器，你知道它们是怎么回事——*砰-砰*。”

En: He says it's important to the case.
Zh: “他说对案子很重要。”

En: Trick question. I'm a martial artist. My entire body is one big gun.
Zh: “偏题了。我是个武术家。我的整个身体就是一把枪。”

En: He says he's in trouble, doesn't have a place to sleep.
Zh: “他说自己有麻烦了，他没地方睡觉。”

En: 10-9, come again please. Over.
Zh: “10-9，再次呼叫。完毕。”

En: Oh, *requests a description*, huh? We'll *give* him one. Describe the Blaasma Gun.
Zh: 哦，*请求描述*，哈？我们会*给*他一个。描述一下那把等离子枪。

En: Uh... He says he is a living weapon and we are trying to trick him.
Zh: “呃……他说他是一个活武器，我们想骗他。”

En: Vicquemare said...
Zh: “维克玛说……”

En: Request denied, sir. Over.
Zh: “请求驳回，先生。完毕。”

En: 10-18. State your message, sir.
Zh: “10-18。请陈述你的信息，先生。”

En: We can do it, brother. You have it in the nerve endings in your hand. You just need to dig into your muscle memory.
Zh: 我们可以做到的，兄弟。它就在你手中的神经末梢。你只需要挖出自己的肌肉记忆。

En: Try to imagine the weapon in your hand.
Zh: 试着想象手中的武器。

En: He's saying he lost his badge.
Zh: “他说自己的警徽不见了。”

En: (Whisper to the lieutenant.) "Kim, what are you packing?"
Zh: （跟警督说悄悄话。）“金，你配的什么枪？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: It's a single-shot Kiejl A9. An *Armistice* to be precise.
Zh: “是一支基耶尔A9单发枪。更准确的说，是一把*止战者*。”

En: Hey, who is this?!
Zh: “喂，你是谁？！”

En: Host in heaven, did he lose his gun *and* his mind?
Zh: “我的神啊，他是把警枪*和*脑子都搞丢了吗？”

En: It's your partner, Satellite-Officer Vicquemare, sir. Over.
Zh: “是你的搭档，随迁警官维克玛，先生。完毕。”

En: He's asking who you are.
Zh: “他问你是谁。”

En: Please, I don't wanna be here... I have to...
Zh: “拜托，我不想待在这里……我要……”

En: Look, I don't have it, all right? I don't have my gun.
Zh: “看，我这里没有，好吗？我的枪没了。”

En: He says it's just a regular gun.
Zh: “他说只是一把普通的枪。”

En: What? What is it?! He's still on the line?!
Zh: “什么？搞什么鬼？！他还没挂断吗？！”

En: Just say you lost your gun. Anything else would only make the situation worse here.
Zh: 就说你把枪弄丢了。说别的只会让情况变得更糟糕。

En: Please just talk human to me. These numbers mean nothing to me.
Zh: “请跟我说人话。这些数字对我来说毫无意义。”

En: Enough with this now, I have other things to discuss.
Zh: “这个说够了，我还有其他的事要讨论。”

En: Satellite-Officer McLaine requests a description of your weapon. Over.
Zh: “随迁警官麦克莱恩要求描述一下你的武器。完毕。”

En: Losing his badge is bad enough. Tell him to find it and *fast*! We can't have some gangbanger running around with it.
Zh: “搞丢警徽已经够糟糕的了。告诉他*赶紧*把东西找回来！我们可不能让青少年罪犯带着它到处跑。”

En: No, I'm alright, I'm all right. I had... other things.
Zh: “不，我没事，我挺好的。我还有……其他事情。”

En: He asked if he ever told me about his days before joining the RCM.
Zh: “他问我，自己有没有说过加入RCM之前的日子。”

En: Ask him... The speaker gasps for air. "Ask him if he lost his *gun* too!" The room roars with laughter.
Zh: “问问他……”说话人喘着气。“问问他是不是把*枪*也搞丢了！”整个房间哄堂大笑。

En: Sergeant Torson wants to know if you lost your gun too. Over.
Zh: “托森警长想知道你是不是把枪也搞丢了。完毕。”

En: Uh... No, sir, I haven't. You're not really keen on mentioning your home life, so I've always assumed things weren't that good on that front. Over.
Zh: “呃……不，先生，我没有。你不太愿意提起自己的家庭生活，所以我一直假设那方面不太顺利。完毕。”

En: Sergeant Torson was wondering if you found your badge yet? Over.
Zh: “托森警长问你有没有找到警徽？完毕。”

En: Yes, tell him I found it from his mother's bed.
Zh: “是的，告诉他，我在他妈的床上找到了。”

En: Alright. The operator turns back to you. "That's a negative on the additional funds, sir. Over."
Zh: “好吧。”接线员回到你这边。“提供额外资金的要求被否决了，先生。完毕。”

En: Any other good news?
Zh: “还有什么好消息吗？”

En: He's not replying. Looks like he is still looking for it. You can hear laughter in the background.
Zh: “他没有回应。看来他还在找。”你听到背景里的笑声更大了。

En: Negative, sir. Over. You can hear laughter in the background.
Zh: “否定，先生。完毕。”你听到背景里的笑声更大了。

En: ... new heights even for Captain Sober!
Zh: “……就算是对于清醒的警长来说，这也是一个全新的高度！”

En: Sir, I will not have you talk to me in this manner. Over.
Zh: “先生，我不允许你这样对我说话。完毕。”

En: Convince them that you didn't lose your gun.
Zh: 让他们相信你的枪没丢。

En: You didn't give an answer.
Zh: 你没有给出回应。

En: You still didn't answer.
Zh: 你还是没有回应。

En: Host allmighty, you still haven't answered anything!
Zh: 天啦，你还是什么也没回应！

En: Come again, sir, I didn't get that! 10-9. Over!
Zh: “请重复一遍，先生，我没听明白！10-9。完毕！”

En: Did he lose his memory along with his fucking badge? The man in the background sounds like he's losing his patience.
Zh: “他的警徽丢了，不会他妈的连记忆也一起跟着丢了吧？”背景里的男声好像有点不耐烦了。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: 10-1, you're breaking up. 10-9, repeat please. Over.
Zh: “10-1，你的信号断了。10-9，请重复。完毕。”

En: Don't say anything.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: I don't want to be here anymore, I want to go home! Please come and get me...
Zh: “我不想再待在这里了，我想回家！请来接我回去……”

En: Remain silent with terror.
Zh: 因恐惧而保持沉默。

En: 10-9, come in, officer! Over.
Come in, officer! 10-9, repeat message please. Did you misplace your firearm? Over.
Zh: “呼叫，警官！10-9，请重复消息。你把枪忘在什么地方了吗？完毕。”

En: Says he lost his badge and needs to report it.
Zh: “他说警徽不见了，需要汇报。”

En: This is Communications Officer Jules Pidieu, sir. Over.
Zh: “我是通讯员朱尔斯·佩迪鲁，先生。完毕。”

En: He says he didn't lose his gun -- *or* his fun, whatever that means.
Zh: “他说他的枪没丢——*而且*姜也没丢，我也不知道是什么意思。”

En: Ask him to describe it! His gun! Not his *fun*, just the gun will do... He laughs.
Zh: “让他描述一下！他的枪！不是他的*姜*，只要枪……”他笑了。

En: State your message, sir. Over.
Zh: “请开始你的汇报，先生。完毕。”

En: Let us address them now, sir. Over.
Zh: “让我们现在就开始吧，先生。完毕。”

En: He says he didn't.
Zh: “他说他没有。”

En: I wanted to know if you got my badge's description right in your report. Could you read it to me -- name, rank, date of birth?
Zh: “我想知道报告里我警徽的信息对不对。你能念给我听一下——名字，职称，还有出生日期？”

En: Who lost his badge?
Zh: “谁的警徽丢了？”

En: Check your pockets! Check your... holy fuck, you don't know where it is, do you?!
Zh: 检查一下你的口袋！检查一下你的……真他妈该死，你不知道它在哪，是吗？！

En: Uh, listen... He seems to lower his voice a bit, carefully choosing his words. "It's okay... you can do it, sir. Over."
Zh: “呃，听着……”他好像稍微压低了一点声音，小心地组织着自己的词汇。“没事的……你可以的，先生。完毕。”

En: Okay, it's gone. Your gun is most definitely gone.
Zh: 好吧，没了。你的枪绝对是没了。

En: Please just say my name, Jules!
Zh: “直接说出来吧，我的名字，朱尔斯！”

En: I need to report my badge missing.
Zh: “我需要汇报一下警徽丢失的事。”

En: 10-10 (Transmission Completed, Standing By). Over. (End call with the 41st.)
Zh: “10-10（传送完毕，等待中）。完毕。”（结束与41分局的通话。）

En: That's all for now. (End call with the 41st.)
Zh: “目前就这些吧。”（结束与41分局的通话。）

En: I am in dire need of financial assistance.
Zh: “我急需财政援助。”

En: Uh... okay, 10-4, sir -- I hear you, relay your question. Over.
Zh: “呃……好吧，10-4，先生——我听见了，传达你的问题。完毕。”

En: Yeah, let's wrap this up. (Conclude.)
Zh: “是啊，到此为止吧。”（总结。）

En: Suddenly the necktie feels like a boa constrictor around your neck, squeezing the dear life out of you.
Zh: 突然间，脖子上的领带感觉就像是一条蟒蛇，想要压榨你宝贵的生命。

En: No, the other one!
Zh: “不，另外一个！”

En: Okay, I heard you -- no funds.
Zh: “好吧，我听到了——没有经费。”

En: Why, did he find it? The room at the other end of the line erupts in volcanic laughter.
Zh: “为什么，他找到了吗？”无线电另一端的房间里爆发出猛烈的笑声。

En: For god's sake, cut this shit out! Tell him to stop wasting time and be a goddamn policeman for a change!
Zh: “看在上帝的份上，少来这套狗屁！告诉他别浪费时间了，改变一下，当个该死的警察吧！”

En: 10-9, come again, I didn't get that. Over. The animated conversation in the back is making it difficult for him to hear you.
Zh: “10-9，再次呼叫，我没听明白。完毕。”背景传来热烈的谈话声，让他很难听见你的声音。

En: He asks you to please stop saying he lost his badge.
Zh: “他问能不能别说弄丢警徽的事了。”

En: He wants to verify the information on his badge.
Zh: “他想核实警徽上的信息。”

En: It is paramount to the investigation that you grant me more money.
Zh: “再多给我点钱，这对调查来说至关重要。”

En: But of course, it says Dick Mullen -- High General of the Revacholian Cavalry Force.
Zh: “当然了，迪克·马伦——瑞瓦肖骑兵队的高级上将如是说。”

En: Could you all please just stop saying 'lost his badge' for a moment?!
Zh: “你们能不能稍微消停一会儿，别说‘弄丢警徽’的话了？！”

En: Wait! Before you say anything stupid, think it through!
Zh: 等等！在你说出什么蠢话之前，先好好考虑一下！

En: Yeah, Mack's right, this was some gnarly shit there. I mean, before he started begging for money -- it was...
Zh: 他右边那个高个子笑的眼泪都没干。“是啊，麦克说得对，妈的痴线。我是说，在他开始开口讨钱的时候——实在是……”

En: Please, I'm begging you here! I don't even have a place to sleep tonight.
Zh: “拜托，我求求你们！我今晚都没地方睡觉了。”

En: He gives you a long concerned look, then nods. "We should get on with our tasks. Work always helps me centre myself. Now... anything else you need from my vehicle or...?"
Zh: 他关切地看了你很长时间，然后点点头。“我们应该继续完成任务。工作总是可以帮我集中注意力。现在……你还需要我車里的什么东西，或者……”

En: What? What is it? What can he possibly still want from us?!
Zh: “什么？这是干什么？他还想从我们这儿得到什么？”

En: 10-4, I hear you. I don't have the authority to grant your request, but....
Zh: “10-4，我听见了。我无权答应你的请求，不过……”

En: He is asking for money.
Zh: “他想要钱。”

En: 10-9. Over.
I don't want to talk about it. Let's just go.
Zh: “我不想提这个。我们走吧。”

En: What, you've never seen a man getting a panic attack before? (Try to keep breathing.)
Zh: “怎么，你之前没见过别人惊恐发作吗？”（试着努力呼吸。）

En: We call it *subterfuge* in the force. Ever heard of it? (Wipe away tears.)
Zh: “我们警察系统都叫这个是*托词*。之前听过吗？”（擦掉眼泪。）

En: 10-9, repeat message. Over.
Zh: “10-9，重复消息。完毕。”

En: Roger that. 10-10. Over and out. With those words the cabin becomes silent again, the radio-microphone resting on its hook.
Zh: “收到。10-10。通话完毕。”话音刚落，整个乘坐舱又陷入沉默，无线话筒也回到了自己的位置上休息。

En: His badge. He lost his goddamned fucking badge.
Zh: “他的警徽。他居然他妈的把该死的警徽弄丢了。”

En: What's going on?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: He lost his *what* now?!
Zh: “现在他又弄丢*什么*了？”

En: What is he saying?
Zh: “他说什么？”

En: What was that? the lieutenant asks in a soft voice, gently grabbing your shoulder. "It's okay, detective."
Zh: “那是怎么回事？”警督的语气很温柔，轻轻地抓住了你的肩膀。“没事的，警探。”

En: Ask him... Ask him to check his hand -- the one without a wine bottle -- I bet that's where his badge is!
Zh: “问问他……”更多笑声传了过来。“让他检查一下自己的手——没拿酒瓶子那只——我打赌警徽就在那里！”

En: You mean your partner? Over.
Zh: “你是说你的搭档吗？完毕。”

En: Supercop here lost his badge!
Zh: “我们的超级警探把自己的警徽搞丢了！”

En: Sir, Satellite-Officer Vicquemare says...
Zh: “先生，随迁警官维克玛说……”

En: Is it *him*?! a dry voice asks in the background. "What does he want?"
Zh: “是*他*吗？！”背景里响起一个干巴巴的声音。“他想干什么？”

En: Is he fucking kidding?!
Zh: “妈的，他在开玩笑吗？！”

En: The small room is filled with cigarette smoke, abuzz with laughter, when Officer Judit Minot enters. Her left arm is in bandages and her hair trimmed short.
Zh: 朱蒂斯·迈诺特警官走进来的时候，整个小房间充满了香烟的味道和笑声。她的左臂打着绷带，头发也剪短了。

En: This is Satellite-Officer McLaine, sir. Over.
Zh: “我是随迁警官麦克莱恩，先生。完毕。”

En: Satellite-Officer Vicquermare concurs, losing your badge *is* serious. Over.
Zh: “随迁警官维克玛表示同意，丢失警徽*真的*很严重。完毕。”

En: Please refer to me with my full name in the future.
Zh: “以后请用全名称呼我。”

En: Satellite-Officer Vicquemare is wondering if you might be joking and adds that this tarnishes the reputation of the entire station. Over.
Zh: “随迁警官维克玛想知道你是不是在开玩笑，还说这样会玷污整个警局的名声。完毕。”

En: He seems intoxicated and keeps asking me to call him by his name.
Zh: “他好像喝醉了，一直让我叫他的全名。”

En: Any news about my... uhm, family?
Zh: “有没有关于……呃，我家人的消息？”

En: The radio falls silent and lifeless. The communications officer has disconnected the call.
Zh: 无线电陷入沉默，死气沉沉的。通讯员断开了电话。

En: I'm the goddamned Hjelmdallermann! Yeah, tell him I hail from the north! His laughter is high-pitched and joyous, almost childlike.
Zh: “我他妈就是来自赫姆达尔的男人！没错，你告诉他，我来自北方！”他的笑声尖锐而喜庆，几乎有点孩子气。

En: My badge, I can't find it anywhere. Basically -- it's gone.
Zh: “我的警徽找不到了。基本上可以说是——不见了。”

En: Of course it's serious. He lost his fucking badge!
Zh: “当然严重了。他把他妈的警徽弄丢了！”

En: Eighteen kilometres to the south, in the 41st Precinct's relay booth, a small crowd has gathered around communication officer Jules "Oldboy" Pidieu -- around a dozen cops.
Zh: 往南18公里，在41分局的休息室里，一小群人聚集在通讯员“老男孩”朱尔斯·佩迪鲁旁边——大概有10多个警员。

En: Oh goddamnit, is he fucking kidding? The whole station's gonna be *dicked* for this.
Zh: “天，他是他妈的开玩笑吗？整个警局都会因此*遭殃*的。”

En: Have I ever told you about my life before the RCM?
Zh: “我有没有跟你说过进入RCM之前的生活？”

En: Fuck me! Mack, come here, you've got to hear this! Dick Mullen lost his badge!
Zh: “我去！麦克，快来，快来听听！迪克·马伦的警徽不见了！”

En: He says he needs money.
Zh: “他说他需要钱。”

En: I don't think he is.
Zh: “我觉得他不是。”

En: Please, I'm scared! I can't... fuck, I can't breathe... Help me, please!
Zh: “拜托，我很害怕！我不能……该死，我不能呼吸了……帮帮我，求你！”

En: He wants to know who you are.
Zh: “他想知道你是谁。”

En: I heard him.
Zh: “我听到他的话了。”

En: He's asking you to stop. Says this is serious.
Zh: “他让你停下来，说这很严重。”

En: He's trying to keep you from further embarrassing yourself in front of your colleagues.
Zh: 他试着让你不要继续在同事面前出丑了。

En: He says this has probably happened to other policemen before him and laughs sarcastically.
Zh: “他说在他之前，其他警探身上可能也发生过这种事情，而且讽刺地笑了。”

En: 10-4, I hear you, officer. I'm just going to make a note here that you are in pursuit of your *misplaced* badge. Over.
Zh: “10-4，我听见了，警官。我这边会留下记录——你正在搜寻*丢失*的警徽。完毕。”

En: Dick fucking Mullen, who do you think?
Zh: “他妈的迪克·马伦，你以为是谁？”

En: Very funny. Who's the Mullen-joker?
Zh: “很好笑。谁是小丑马伦？”

En: C'mon operator, tell them to stop. This is serious!
Zh: “拜托，通讯员，让他们停下来。这事很严重！”

En: Ha-ha, officer has lost his badge, ha-ha. Like I'm the first cop to ever misplace his badge.
Zh: “哈哈，警官的警徽不见了，哈哈。搞得好像我是第一个弄丢警徽的警察一样。”

En: Is it *him*?! a dry voice asks in the background. "What does he want?"
Zh: “是*他*吗？！”背景里响起一个干巴巴的声音。“他想干什么？”

En: Tell him to stop wasting time!
Zh: “让他别再浪费时间了！”

En: Can't we just move on? I want to get it reported and be done with it. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我们就不能先继续吗？我就想做个汇报，然后就结束了。”（继续。）

En: Did you want anything else, sir? He chooses to ignore the voice in the background. "*Many* of your colleagues are also here. Over."
Zh: 通讯员选择忽略背景里的声音。“还有什么需要的吗，先生？”他询问到。“你有*很多*同事也在这里。完毕。”

En: He... he terminated the connection. The radio operator is no longer speaking to the microphone and is addressing someone in the background. "I guess he was in a hurry..."
Zh: “他……他结束通话了。”通讯员拿开麦克风，扭头看着背景里的某个人。“我猜他很着急……”

En: You're going to be looking at a straight jacket if you tell everyone you lost your memory. Be smart about this! Ask if he's there alone.
Zh: 如果你告诉大家自己失忆了，肯定会被当成一个穿着束身衣的疯子。放聪明点！问问他是不是一个人。

En: Try to loosen its death-grip and breathe.
Zh: 试着松开它的死亡之握，努力呼吸。

En: Don't give that asshole anything, he's just gonna drink it all!
Zh: “千万别给那个混帐东西，他肯定又会拿去喝酒的！”

En: 10-4, message received. This is a *very* serious situation. I need to 10-22 the captain. Over.
Zh: “10-4，收到消息。这是*非常*严重的情况。我需要汇报给10-22警长。完毕。”

En: What the hell is happening to me?!
Zh: “我到底发生什么事了？！”

En: What is he saying?
Zh: “他在说什么？”

En: It's officer Dick Mullen from the bestseller 'Dick Mullen and the Lost Identity,' he says fighting off laughter.
Zh: “是迪克警官……”他努力忍着笑说到，“……那本畅销书《迪克·马伦与丢失的身份》里的马伦。”

En: Mullen's drunk and emotionally aggressive. That's new.
Zh: “马伦喝醉酒，情绪有些激动。这还挺新鲜的。”

En: Hey, if you don't want to have the most *intense orgasm* of your life, fine -- don't! The grip eases a little.
Zh: 喂，如果你想感受人生中最*激烈的高潮*，那好吧——就不要了！紧缚感稍微放松了一些。

En: It's officer Dick.... he tries to speak through laughter, "... Mullen from the bestseller 'Dick Mullen and the Lost Identity.'"
Zh: “是迪克警官……”他努力忍着笑说到，“……那本畅销书《迪克·马伦与丢失的身份》里的马伦。”

En: Ask him... The speaker succumbs to laughter. "Ask him to check his hand -- the one without a wine bottle -- I bet that's where his badge is!"
Zh: “问问他……”更多笑声传了过来。“让他检查一下自己的手——没拿酒瓶子那只——我打赌警徽就在那里！”

En: Suddenly he interjects: "Yeah, Mullen was fucked alright. Sounded fucking drunk to me."
Zh: 他突然插嘴进来：“是啊，马伦他妈的搞砸了。我听起来应该是他妈的喝醉了。”

En: The tall ginger on his right still has tears of laughter in his eyes. "Yeah, Mack's right, this was some gnarly shit there. I mean, before he started begging for money -- it was... "
Zh: 他右边那个高个子笑的眼泪都没干。“是啊，麦克说得对，妈的痴线。我是说，在他开始开口讨钱的时候——实在是……”

En: The tall ginger on his right still has tears of laughter in his eyes. "Yeah, Mack's right, that was some gnarly shit there..."
Zh: 他右边那个高个子笑的眼泪还没擦干。“是啊，麦克说得对，妈的痴线……”

En: What's there to think about?
Zh: 有什么好考虑的？

En: I need *information*, not fear.
Zh: 我需要*信息*，不是恐惧。

En: Be smart about this, ask if he's there alone.
Zh: 放聪明点，问问他是不是一个人。

En: I have successfully located my 9mm Villiers pistol. It's on me now and I won't lose it again.
Zh: “我已经成功找回我的维利耶9毫米手枪了。现在它就在我身上，我再也不会搞丢了。”

En: 10-4, sir. Roger that. And very glad to hear it. I will make relevant changes to the report.
Zh: “10-4，先生。收到。很高兴听到这个消息。我会在报告做出相应的修改。”

En: Jules, I've heard that some people think of me as a La Puta Madre's peone... Do you think I'm corrupted?
Zh: “朱尔斯，我听说有人以为我是马德雷教父的爪牙……你觉得我是不是受贿了？”

En: 10-4, sir. There's a pause, as he seems to mull it over behind his enormous radio-microphone. "Well, there's been some talk, sir," he finally says reluctantly.
Zh: “10-4，先生。”对方停顿了一下，好像在巨大的无线电麦克风后沉默地思考着。“呃，确实有些传言，先生，”他最后还是不情愿地开了口。

En: I only meant that there's been some talk in the station, that's all. But there's always some talk in the station. You know how officers in Jamrock are...
Zh: “我的意思是说，只是警局内部有些传言，就这些。不过警局里一直都有各种各样的传言。你也知道加姆洛克的警官什么样……”

En: I only meant that there's been some talk in the station, that's all. But there's always some talk in the station. You know how officers in Jamrock are...
Zh: “我的意思是说，只是警局内部有些传言，就这些。不过警局里一直都有各种各样的传言。你也知道加姆洛克的警官什么样……”

En: Sylvie? I believe we've met before. This is me, a detective from the Whirling-in-Rags!
Zh: “塞尔维？我相信我们之前见过。是我，褴褛飞旋的警探！”

En: Ouch.... That's like twenty points of pride-damage right there, buddy.
Zh: 好痛……就好像你的自尊受到了20点的伤害，伙计。

En: He told me he asked you out. Are you saying it didn't happen?
Zh: “他说他邀请你出去约会。你是说这事没发生吗？”

En: So you do agree that quitting your job just because someone asked you out is an *overreaction*?
Zh: “所以你也同意，只是因为有人邀请你出去约会就辞职有点*反应过度*了？”

En: You mean me? You needed to get away from *me*?
Zh: “你是说我吗？你需要远离*我*吗？”

En: I *really* don't want to talk about this. Let's just forget about this, okay?
Zh: “我*真的*不想提。忘了这个话题，好吗？”

En: Sounds like it's going to be bad. Do you really want to know?
Zh: 听起来应该挺糟糕的。你真想知道吗？

En: It almost looked like you pawned it, but believe me, I did *not* ask. She sighs.
Zh: “看起来你像是把它当掉了，但是相信我，我*没有*过问。”她叹了口气。

En: There is *no* resentment in her tone. She wants you to ask her out. No question about it.
Zh: 她的语气中并没有*怨恨*。她想等你先开口，这一点是毫无疑问的。

En: Garbage!
Zh: 屁话！

En: Push her *further*. Show her the error of her ways.
Zh: 再*加把劲*推她。让她明白自己的错误。

En: So the Union has a phone -- and there's one further down the coast. Got it.
Zh: 所以工会有一台电话——海岸边也有一台。明白了。

En: It was someone else. The lieutenant makes a note. "We'll find them sooner or later, officer. It just might take a while."
Zh: “是其他人。”警督做了笔记。“我们迟早会找到他们的，警官。只是可能要费点时间。”

En: You have to ask someone else. There are thousands of people in Martinaise.
Zh: “你得去问问其他人。马丁内斯有好几千人呢。”

En: Relax. You'll find them sooner or later -- this will just take some time.
Zh: 放松。你迟早会找到他们的——只是需要花点时间。

En: Am I, though?
Zh: 不过，我真是吗？

En: Rubbish! You're a green hat and feather totin' birdwatcher!
Zh: 屁话！你是个戴着绿色羽毛帽子的观鸟人！

En: Or a cop with brain damage. One of the two.
Zh: 或者是脑损伤的警察。二者之一。

En: I won't even *mention* you waving your gun around, harassing customers, *threatening* to sing karaoke, threatening to kill yourself...
Zh: “我甚至还没*提到*你拿着枪到处晃，打扰顾客，*恐吓*说要唱卡拉OK，还威胁说要自杀的事……”

En: Interesting... you still have to find it, however.
Zh: 有点意思……不过你还是要找到它。

En: Seems like Klaasje wasn't lying when she said it was her. The lieutenant makes a note. "This corroborates her story -- she made the call."
Zh: “看起来卡拉洁没有说谎，就是她。”警督记下笔记。“这就证实了她的故事——是她打的电话。”

En: Seems like Klaasje wasn't lying when she said she made the call. This corroborates.
Zh: 看起来卡拉洁没有说谎，就是她打的电话。正好证实了这一点。

En: She iseth the fairest of them all. The ugliness of lies wouldst never escape her lips!
Zh: 她是所有人中最诚实的那一个。丑陋的谎言永远不会从她的嘴里说出来！

En: Are you *sure* lieutenant? For some reason, you have a little doubt about that...
Zh: 你*确定*吗，警督？出于某种原因，你有一点点怀疑……

En: I wouldn't be so sure, detective.
Zh: 我不会这么肯定，警探。

En: Interesting... you still have to find a copy though. Before you can blast it.
Zh: 有点意思……不过你还需要找到一份拷贝——在你毁了它之前。
