En: Ah yes, *that* paperwork. I found it.
Zh: “啊，是的，*那份*文件。我找到了。”

En: Good. Then it's not lost. She seems a little relieved. "Just don't throw it in the toilet again. Toilets don't work like that."
Zh: “很好。那就没有丢。”她好像稍微宽慰了些。“别再把它扔进马桶里了。马桶也不喜欢这样的。”

En: Especially when there's a hurricane loose. It's *your* fault for losing them -- not mine.
Zh: “特别是你的房间像被台风刮过一样。弄丢它们是*你*的错——不是我的。”

En: Something in you wants to immediately *forget* about this. As if there was a reason you threw them away.
Zh: 你心里有些东西想让你立即*忘掉*这一切。就好像你扔掉它们是有理由。

En: Are you *sure* you don't remember where the paperwork I flushed is?
Zh: “你*确定*不记得我把文件冲到什么地方了？”

En: Sorry about that. She doesn't sound like she's too sorry. "Anything else, detective?"
Zh: “我很抱歉。”听起来她不是真的抱歉。“还有什么事吗，警探？”

En: Yes, I'm sure. Every time we talk about it I'm going to be *more* sure I don't know.
Zh: “是的，我确定。每次我们谈到这个的时候，我都会*更加*确定自己不知道。”

En: I, ugh... the ones I had to wrench out of your toilet?
Zh: “我，呃……是我从你马桶里掏出来的那些吗？”

En: What happened to them?
Zh: “它们怎么了？”

En: I... dammit, I don't remember what I did to your damn papers! I don't remember every little thing I do... Resentment gives way to concern in her voice.
Zh: “我……见鬼，我不记得对你那些该死的文件做了什么！不是每件小事我都能记得的……”她声音中的怨恨被关心取代了。

En: She's worried she might have accidentally done something bad. Then she remembers it's *you*.
Zh: 她担心自己可能会不小心做了什么坏事。然后她记起来是*你*。

En: Especially when there's a hurricane loose. It's your fault for losing them -- not mine.
Zh: “特别是台风要来的时候。弄丢它们是你的错——不是我的。”

En: Something in you wants to immediately *forget* about this. As if there was a reason you threw them away.
Zh: 你身体里有某种东西叫嚣着，想让你*忘掉*这一切。就好像你扔掉它们是有理由。

En: I put them in my toilet?
Zh: “我把它们丢进厕所的？”

En: Yes. You put *something* there. Causing bad water damage in the kitchen. I think it was that.
Zh: “是的。你把*什么东西*丢进去了。搞得厨房严重漏水。我想应该就是那个。”

En: It's *on*. The Love Quest is on -- too late everyone! It's *on*. Take it to Garte now!
Zh: *开始*了。恋爱任务开始了——太迟了，大家！已经*开始*了。现在去找加尔特吧！

En: God, why can't you just mind your own business... she mutters.
Zh: “老天，为什么你就不能只管自己的事呢……”她嘟囔着。

En: Sorry. Sorry about the song.
Zh: “抱歉。那首歌的事，我很抱歉。”

En: To hell with that song...
Zh: “让那首歌见鬼去吧……”

En: I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: Why not?
Zh: 为什么不呢？

En: If she used to work in the Whirling-in-Rags, then you must have met her.
Zh: 如果她曾经在褴褛飞旋工作，那你肯定见过她。

En: Oh, all sorts of things. Some disco, rock too. So much disco and rock...
Zh: “哦，各种各样的东西。有迪斯科，还有摇滚。太多迪斯科和摇滚了……”

En: Okay. I don't know what to say.
Zh: “好吧。我不知道该说什么。”

En: Me neither.
Zh: “我也不知道。”

En: Whatever this Empathy failure tells you -- *don't* take the quest. It's not wonderful, it's ugly.
Zh: 不论共情跟你说什么——*不要*接下这个任务。它一点也不好，它很难看。

En: That's where *you* step in. You're Lieutenant Love, matchmaker extraordinaire! Help the poor girl out, lest she turns into a *spinster*.
Zh: 这就是*你*要介入的地方。你是爱情警督，超级红娘！帮帮那个可怜的女孩吧，免得她变成一个*老姑娘*！

En: Oh my god, not a spinster!
Zh: 我的天啦，老姑娘可不行！

En: Yeah. She's a woman. Probably just playing hardball with the goods. Women are just transactional.
Zh: 是啊。她是个女人。也许只是对商品的态度比较强硬。女人是很看中交易的。

En: What am I, deranged? A woman doesn't have to be married. She doesn't even have to be in a relationship. Also, feminism.
Zh: 我这是怎么回事，精神错乱了？谁说女人一定要结婚。她甚至都不需要谈恋爱。又是女权主义。

En: I'm happy to help, but maybe I could do so without all this internalized misogyny?
Zh: 我很愿意帮忙，不过如果没有这些内在化的厌女症，也许也能行？

En: What misogyny? I'm just telling things the way they are. Can't a man be honest in his *own head* anymore?
Zh: 什么厌女症？我只是就事论事。难道男人就不能在*自己的头脑*里诚实一点吗？

En: Not a spinster, my god!
Zh: 绝对不能变成老姑娘，我的天！

En: What *deranged*? I'm just telling things the way they are. Can't a man even be honest in his *own head* anymore?
Zh: 什么*精神错乱*？我只是就事论事。难道男人就不能在*自己的头脑*里诚实一点吗？

En: You have to intervene on behalf of the Ethnic Nation of Love (Equally of Sun and Lightning)! First calm her down, tell her you've got everything *under control* -- and then go have a boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 作为爱情民族国度（同样代表太阳和闪电）的代表，你必须介入其中！首先让她冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: You have to intervene on behalf of the People's Republic of Love! First calm her down, tell her you've got everything *under control* -- and then go have a boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 作为爱情共和国的代表，你必须介入其中！首先让她冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: You have to intervene on behalf of the International Lovetary Fund! First calm her down, tell her you've got everything *under control* -- and then go have a boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 作为国际爱情基金组织的代表，你必须介入其中！首先让她冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: You have to intervene on behalf of the Combined Ministry of Love and Education! First calm her down, tell her you've got everything *under control* -- and then go have a boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 作为爱情与教育综合部的代表，你必须介入其中！首先让她冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: You have to intervene on behalf of Love! First calm her down, tell her you've got everything *under control* -- and then go have a boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 你必须以爱情的名义介入其中！首先让她冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: Think you can do that, Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Love?\n
Zh: 你能做到吗，双重荣誉爱情警督？

En: Think you can do that, Lieutenant Love?
Zh: 你能做到吗，爱情警督？

En: Khm... (To the microphone.) "Are you still there, kiddo? Listen... I've got everything under control."
Zh: “嗯……”（对着扩音器。）“你还在吗，姑娘？听着……一切都在我的掌控之中。”

En: No. What?
Zh: “不。什么？”

En: You and Garte, right? A little trouble under the sheets? Say no more, papa's got this. Big Poppa.
Zh: “你和加尔特，对吧？遇到了一点小问题？不用多说，都交给老爹吧。大老爹我。”

En: Oh yeah. My point exactly. You know how they are -- transactional and hysteric. Loony broads.
Zh: 哦耶。这就是我的观点。你知道是怎么回事——交易型，而且还歇斯底里。疯婆子。

En: You know what you've got to do. First you've got to calm her down, tell her you've got everything *under control* -- then go and have a little boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 你知道自己该做什么。首先让她冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: Daddy is going to take you on his lap, little darling.
Zh: “老爹要带你装逼带你飞，小宝贝。”

En: Please no. I don't want to say any of those things. (Refuse the Love Quest -- although it's *wonderful*.)
Zh: 请不要这样了。我不想再说这种话。（拒绝这个爱情任务——虽然真的感觉*很棒*。）

En: Oh god. The static appears to breathe heavily. "Please just stay out of my li..."
Zh: “天啦。”对方的呼吸开始变得沉重。“离我远一点……”

En: I know, baby, I know. It's just that you've got that sweet woman-child thing that makes men go *crazy*. Especially a man like Garte.
Zh: “我知道的，宝贝，我懂。只是你身上有一种可爱的女孩子家家的东西，能让男人为之*疯狂*。特别是加尔特那种男人。”

En: You have to *act*, Lieutenant Love. You have to calm that hysteric down, tell it you've got everything under control -- then go and have a little boys' talk with Garte himself.
Zh: 你必须*行动*起来，爱情警督。首先你必须让这个歇斯底里的女人冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: You're just a gimp. What, you want to be more *empathetic*?
Zh: 你只是个瘸子。怎么，你想变得更*通情达理*一些？

En: Nothing's wrong, it's just *sssssexuality*.
Zh: 没什么不对的，这只是人类的*原始欲望*。

En: You have to *act*, Lieutenant Love. You have to calm that hysteric girl down, tell her you've got everything under control -- and then go and have a little boys' talk with the man himself.
Zh: 你必须*行动*起来，爱情警督。首先你必须让这个歇斯底里的女人冷静下来，告诉她一切都在你的*掌控*之中——然后跟加尔特来一场男人之间的对话。

En: Khm... Correction: Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Love.
Zh: 咳咳……纠正：双重荣誉爱情警督。

En: Stop! That's Lieutenant Double-Yefreitor Love for you.
Zh: 住嘴！你才是双重荣誉爱情警督。

En: Of course. So much more love. But now -- react!
Zh: 当然。还有更多的爱。不过现在——行动起来吧！

En: God -- what is happening in your HEAD?!
Zh: 我的天——你的脑子到底出什么事了？！

En: Big Big Poppa is happening.
Zh: 发生大事了。

En: Was I singing... The Smallest Church In Saint-Saëns"?"
Zh: “我有没有唱过……‘圣桑小教堂’？”

En: Yes. That's the one you liked to sing along to the *most*. The later it got the more *that one* came on.
Zh: “有啊。这是你*最喜欢*唱的一首歌了。时间越晚，*那个*出现的就越多。”

En: What else did I sing beside the OO? I'm looking for a song...
Zh: “除了招摇乐团的歌之外，我还唱了别的什么吗？我在找一首歌……”

En: You said I was singing. What did I sing? I'm looking for a song...
Zh: “你说我在唱歌，我唱的是什么？我在找一首歌……”

En: All sorts of things. The OO. Some disco, rock too. So much disco and rock...
Zh: “各种各样的歌。招摇乐团。迪斯科，摇滚也有。迪斯科和摇滚有点太多了……”

En: The lieutenant goes pale. 'Missing badge?' he mouths to himself, silently.
Zh: 警督面色苍白。“警徽不见了？”他默默地自言自语到。

En: Hello, this is the police calling. I have some questions for you about your last days at work.
Zh: “喂，我是警察。我想问问你前几天工作的事。”

En: Oh, right... She recognizes your voice almost immediately. "Hello, officer, what can I do for you?"
Zh: “哦，好吧……”她几乎是瞬间就认出了你的声音。“你好，警官，有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: You can hear resentment in her tone -- she's not thrilled to be talking to you again.
Zh: 你可以听出她语气里的愤懑——她一点也不想再次跟你通话。

En: Wait, before you go... You're mad at me, right? Tell me, what did I do? I can't remember anything.
Zh: “等等，先别挂……你很生我的气，对吗？告诉我，我做了什么？我什么也记不清了。”

En: Hey, do you want to grab a cup of coffee with me some time?
Zh: “喂，你想不想抽个时间跟我一起喝杯咖啡？”

En: No, absolutely not.
Zh: “不，绝对不要。”

En: I think I got everything I need, thanks. [End call.]
Zh: “我想我已经得到所有必要信息了，谢谢。”[结束通话。]

En: I do hope so. Please don't call me again. Bye. She's ready to hang up.
Zh: “希望如此。请不要再打给我了。再见。”她准备挂断电话。

En: I'm not mad, it's just... The static sighs again. "You were so drunk and so emotional all the time, and then the *skua thing* happened. It just made me want to quit."
Zh: “我没有生气，只是……”对方又叹了口气。“你总是喝得醉醺醺的，然后又很情绪化，然后就发生了*贼鸥*的事。让我很想辞职。”

En: What skua thing?
Zh: “什么贼鸥的事？”

En: The stuffed bird. The great skua. You threw it against the wall, while screaming 'fuck that bird' and laughing like a maniac.
Zh: “鸟类标本。大贼鸥。你一边把它扔到墙上，一边喊着‘去死吧小鸟’，然后又像个疯子一样大笑。”

En: I think you said it had been *giving you shit ever since you got there*.
Zh: “我想你好像说过：*自从你去了那里之后，它就一直在朝你头上拉屎*之类的话。”

En: This sounds like me alright.
Zh: “听起来确实很像我说过的话。”

En: This doesn't sound anything like me, I love birds!
Zh: “听起来一点都不像我，我很爱小鸟的！”

En: Bitch bird got what was coming to her.
Zh: “那只贱鸟，是它罪有应得。”

En: Why do I always end up screwing everything up?
Zh: “为什么最后我总是把事情搞砸呢？”

En: Didn't seem like you had fun doing it though.
Zh: “不过，看起来你也不是很开心。”

En: So you're telling me that *I* was the one who made you want to quit?
Zh: “所以你是想说，是*我*让你想辞职的吗？”

En: Go on. I want to know what I did.
Zh: “继续。我想知道我做了什么。”

En: I think I've heard enough. (Back off.)
Zh: “我想我已经听的够多了。”（让步。）

En: I bet.
Zh: “我猜也是。”

En: Well... You were worse than all of them. Honestly, you were getting borderline aggressive.
Zh: “呃……你比他们所有人更糟糕。老实说，你已经到达咄咄逼人的边界了。”

En: Even about little things, like not turning down the volume at 3 AM. I even *liked* one of those songs you kept listening to on repeat. No more. I hate it now.
Zh: “就算是小事也一样，比如凌晨3点也不肯把声音关小。我甚至还*喜欢*上你一直听的某首歌了。不过已经过去了。现在我很讨厌它。”

En: Right. Was that all?
Zh: “好吧。就这些吗？”

En: Then there was your room. Your *project*, an experiment to see how bad it can get in there.
Zh: “然后就是你的房间。你的*计划*，一场想看看到底能有多糟糕的实验。”

En: I tried to send the cleaner, but you wouldn't let me. Threatened to 'make me understand'. I had no idea what you meant -- and I *don't* want to know.
Zh: “我想叫清洁工，但你不让。威胁说要‘让我明白’。我不知道你什么意思——而且我也*不想*知道。”

En: And then you screamed something about how you're actually a 'real cool guy' and no one understands it... 'One of the coolest guys there is'. *The* coolest guy in Jamrock. Something about disco too...
Zh: “然后你开始尖叫，说你其实真的是个‘很酷的家伙’，但是没人理解……说自己是‘有史以来最酷的人’。加姆洛克*最酷*的仔。还说了跟迪斯科有关的话……”

En: And then you screamed something about being the most boring person alive and how everyone leaves you 'cause you're just so *booooring*.
Zh: “然后你开始尖叫，说自己是最无趣的人类，所有人都离开你了，‘因为你真的是太太太*无趣*了。”

En: And then you screamed something about how you are a piece of shit human being and why does anyone even let you work as a police man -- that you'd fire yourself, but you can't even do that...
Zh: “然后你开始尖叫，说自己是一坨狗屎，为什么会有人让你当警察——说你会炒了自己，但是就连这个都做不到……”

En: And then you screamed something about the end of all times and how everyone is a piece of shit. She takes a deep breath.
Zh: “然后你开始尖叫，说什么末日要到了，所有人都是一坨屎什么的。”她深深地吸了一口气。

En: And then you screamed something incomprehensible about churches and the scent of apricots and aquariums. She takes a deep breath.
Zh: “还有，你大声嚷嚷着一些教堂，杏子味，还有鱼缸之类让人费解的话。”她深吸一口气。

En: Okay, I get it. I wasn't a very good tenant.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了。我不是个好住客。”

En: No, you really weren't. You were simply the worst.
Zh: “不，你真的不是。你就是最糟糕的那一个。”

En: I am truly sorry for everything, Sylvie.
Zh: “我真心为这一切向你道歉，塞尔维。”

En: I was trying to show you the world of tomorrow. The Great Panic at The End.
Zh: “我是想向你展示明天的世界。末日大恐慌。”

En: Well, you're the worst tavern wench I've ever seen!
Zh: “呃，你是我见过的糟糕的酒馆招待！”

En: God, I... I knew I shouldn't have brought it up. Just... try not to call me again and let's pretend it never happened.
Zh: “天，我……我知道不该提起这件事。只是……别再打给我了，我们就假装这一切从来没有发生。”

En: Girl, just loosen up a little... Don't you ever party?
Zh: “姑娘，稍微放松一点点……你难道没派对过吗？”

En: Anything else I can help you with, officer?
Zh: “还有什么能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: You hear the call breaking up on the other end of the radio and then the already familiar voice:
Zh: 你听到对方挂断了电话，然后是一道熟悉的声音：

En: (Test Klaasje's story.) "Was it you who called the police?"
Zh: （验证卡拉洁的故事。）“是你打电话报的警吗？”

En: Was it you who called the police?
Zh: “是你打电话报警的吗？”

En: No, not me.
Zh: “不，不是我。”

En: But why *didn't* you call? Didn't a corpse behind your workplace bother you?
Zh: “那你*为什么*没报警呢？你不觉得工作的地方后面有具尸体很困扰吗？”

En: Okay, next question. (Back.)
Zh: “好吧，下一个问题。”（返回。）

En: Do you know who made that call?
Zh: “你知道是谁报警的吗？”

En: No, sorry... I don't. She clears her throat. "Not a lot of people have phones around here. Copper thieves take the wires..."
Zh: “不，抱歉……我不知道。”她清了清喉咙。“这里有电话的人不多。铜线小偷把电话线剪走了……”

En: What? Of course it bothered me! But I thought the Union already knew about the corpse.
Zh: “什么？我当然困扰啊！但是我觉得工会已经知道尸体的事了。”

En: I meant us. You should have called *the police*.
Zh: “我是指我们。你应该打给*警察*。”

En: No one calls the police. You can hear her adjust the receiver in her hand. "The Union would get angry."
Zh: “没人会报警的。”你可以听出她调整了一下手中的听筒。“因为工会的人会生气。”

En: Looks like there's a limit to my authority then.
Zh: “看来我的权威也是有限度的。”

En: I see. Maybe there's something else you can tell me about? (Back off.)
Zh: “我明白了。也许你可以跟我说点别的？”（让步。）

En: I don't know what to tell you, officer. I didn't call you, because I didn't want to get in trouble with the others, with the Union. I'm sorry about that.
Zh: “我不知道有什么可以说的，警官。我没打给你，是因为我不想招惹别人，招惹工会的人。这一点我很抱歉。”

En: Tell me, why *exactly* did you let a corpse hang in your own backyard for weeks instead of calling us? (Push on.)
Zh: “告诉我，你为什么宁愿让一具尸体在后院里挂上好几个星期也不给我们打电话呢？”（继续追问。）

En: I... I didn't want to get in trouble with the others...
Zh: “我……我不想惹上什么麻烦……”

En: No, don't push her. It sounds as if she's about to cry.
Zh: 不，别逼她。听起来她好像要哭了。

En: I see. Don't worry about it, I understand. (Back off.)
Zh: “明白。别担心，我能理解。”（让步。）

En: Let me get this straight, you ignored the law to save your own skin?
Zh: “我就直说了吧，你是为了保全自己的性命而选择无视法律吗？”

En: You do? She sounds relieved. "What else can I do for you?"
Zh: “是吗？”她听起来松了口气。“还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: I... I didn't know I had to report it... I... I thought someone would take him down eventually... Her voice breaks.
Zh: “我……我不知道我必须上报……我……我以为最后总会有人把他弄下来的……”她的声音支离破碎。

En: It's okay, things happen. Not really your fault.
Zh: “没事的，事情总会发生。这不是你的错。”

En: It's a little late for tears, isn't it? Should have used them to summon the police earlier.
Zh: “现在哭已经有点太晚了，不是吗？你应该早点用眼泪把警察招来的。”

En: Yes... I know, it's just... She takes a deep breath before continuing. "Was there anything else?"
Zh: “是的……我知道，只是……”她深深吸了一口气，然后继续说了下去。“还有什么事吗？”

En: You can almost hear the girl getting smaller on the other end of the line, until she almost drowns in static.
Zh: 电话线那头女孩的声音几乎越变越小，最后几乎要淹没在静电声中。

En: No... You can hear her regaining control in the background. "I honestly don't know."
Zh: “不……”你可以听出背景里的她又重新夺回了控制权。“我真的不知道。”

En: Yeah, go on.
Zh: “好吧，继续。”

En: Okay... She's calmer now. Probably because the focus has shifted elsewhere.
Zh: “好吧……”现在她冷静多了。也许是因为注意力转移到了其他地方。

En: You quit your job at the Whirling -- why?
Zh: “你辞了飞旋旅社的工作——为什么？”

En: Have you seen my badge?
Zh: “你见过我的警徽吗？”

En: Have you seen my gun?
Zh: “你见过我的枪吗？”

En: I showed you my gun? When did it happen?
Zh: “我把枪给你看了？什么时候的事？”

En: Yes, I know who you are. You're a police officer... *the law*.
Zh: “是的，我知道你是谁。你是一个警官……*你就是法律*。”

En: My badge is missing. Have you seen it anywhere?
Zh: “我的警徽不见了。你有没有在哪里见过？”

En: Oh... no, I haven't, sorry.
Zh: “哦……不，我没见过，抱歉。”

En: Please, no. Not this again -- everyone saw your 'cool gun', detective... She sounds beyond exacerbated.
Zh: “拜托了，不要。不要又来一次——人人都看见你那把‘酷炫的枪’了，警探……”她的声音已经出离愤怒了。

En: You were trying to *impress* some people with it. Everyone was eating and... She stops hesitantly, not sure if she should continue.
Zh: “你想要用它*打动*某些人。大家都在吃饭，然后……”她停了下来，欲言又止地，不确定自己该不该继续。

En: No idea. All I know is, next you were waving around money instead. Saying things like 'Big bucks cannot lie' and 'Guns can't buy money, but money can always buy guns'.
Zh: “不知道。我只知道接下来你又开始挥舞着手中的钞票。然后喊着什么‘钱是不会撒谎的’，还有什么‘枪买不来钱，但是钱永远可以拿来买枪’。”

En: Hey, I found my patrol cloak earlier, but have you seen my police man uniform?
Zh: “喂，我刚才找到自己的巡逻斗篷了，但是你有没有见过我的警察制服？”

En: Have you seen my police man uniform?
Zh: “你见到我的警服了吗？”

En: Uniform? I never saw you in any uniform, you had your... *things* on. The disco things.
Zh: “警服？我从没见你穿过警服，你穿着你自己的……*衣服*。迪斯科之类的。”

En: It feels like you haven't worn yours in a long-long time.
Zh: 感觉你应该很久很久没穿自己那套了。

En: Wait, why does she seem angry with you?
Zh: 等等，为什么她好像对你很恼火？

En: She's hiding something.
Zh: 她有所隐瞒。

En: Quick to tell you off.
Zh: 快点告诉你。

En: Garte asked her out.
Zh: 加尔特约她出去。

En: Wanted to get away from you.
Zh: 想躲开你。

En: Made her cry earlier.
Zh: 之前把她惹哭过。

En: Said you're the authority.
Zh: 说过你是当局的人。

En: You asked her out.
Zh: 你约过她出去。

En: Rigorous Self-Critique.
Zh: 严格的自我批判

En: Inexplicable Feminist Agenda.
Zh: 令人费解的女权主义议程。

En: Bye, Sylvie.
Zh: “再见，塞尔维。”

En: I got a hunch your love life is about to take a *very* pleasant turn.
Zh: “我有种预感，你的感情生活将会发生一个*非常*愉快的转折。”

En: Call was terminated by the other party. Anything else, officer?
Zh: “对方终止了通话。还有其他事吗，警官？”

En: I've got a hunch your love life is about to take a *very* pleasant turn. (Give the lieutenant a knowing wink.)
Zh: “我有种预感，你的感情生活将会发生一个*非常*愉快的转折。”（朝警督会意地眨眨眼。）

En: What? The lieutenant raises his shoulders. "What is it?"
Zh: “什么？”警督抬起肩膀。“这是干什么？”

En: You'll see, lieutenant. You will see. (Wink again.)
Zh: “你会明白的，警督。你会的。”（再次眨眨眼。）

En: Yes. You have obviously done something to upset her -- at the Whirling-in-Rags, when she was still working there.
Zh: 是的。你显然做了什么让她很不安的事——当她还在褴褛飞旋工作的时候。

En: No. She doesn't have a problem with you. It must be someone *else* she's angry about. Some other guy... like *Garte*.
Zh: 不。她跟你之间没有什么问题。她肯定是在生*其他人*的气。其他男人……比如说*加尔特*。

En: Yeah, it's definitely some other guy, a guy like *Garte*.
Zh: 是啊，肯定是其他人，比如说*加尔特*。

En: Uh, okay... but I'm a guy. Sure it isn't about me?
Zh: 啊，好吧……但我也是个男人。确定不是我吗？

En: Trust me, you wouldn't want to be *the guy* here. You know how it is. Yack yack nag nag.
Zh: 相信我，你肯定不想成为*那个人*。你知道是怎么回事。叽叽喳喳的。

En: You know women and their constant *problems*. Yack yack nag nag. How's one supposed to get *the love* going like that?
Zh: 你懂女人，还有她们没完没了的*问题*。叽叽喳喳的。怎么能有人让*爱情*那样继续下去呢？

En: No, you're The Guy  -- you're Lieutenant Love, matchmaker extraordinaire! Help the poor girl out, lest she turns into a *spinster*!
Zh: 不，你就是那个人——你是爱情警督，超级红娘！帮帮那个可怜的女孩吧，免得她变成一个*老姑娘*！

En: Maybe you can help me identify this one particular song...
Zh: “也许你能帮我认出其中的一个首歌……”

En: Which one would that be?
Zh: “哪一首？”

En: One that was really sad, about a small church...?
Zh: “那首特别悲伤的，好像讲的是一座小教堂……？”

En: One that was really *sad*.
Zh: “那首特别特别*悲伤*的。”

En: Sad? I think the one you mean is The Smallest Church in Saint-Saëns". Butchered that right up..." Her voice carries a tone of disappointment.
Zh: “悲伤？我想你说的应该是‘圣桑小教堂’。直到把它毁掉了……”她的声音带着一丝失望。

En: What *others*?
Zh: “*其他人*是谁？”

En: The other people who live around here. Local people. I... I didn't want trouble...
Zh: “住在这附近的其他人。当地居民。我……我不想惹麻烦……”

En: That came so fast you can't but wonder what else would she be good at.
Zh: 她回答得太快了，让你不禁只能好奇她到底还擅长些什么。

En: Yes, what would she be good at?
Zh: 没错，她擅长什么呢？

En: Baseball? Ring-a-bell Quiz Shows? Catching keys in the air? Petting an angry cat?
Zh: 棒球？打架？问答节目？空中抓钥匙？安抚炸毛的猫？

En: Her voice is resigned and weak.
Zh: 她的声音顺从而虚弱。

En: Mhmh.
Hey, Sylvie, it's the police again.
Zh: “喂，塞尔维，又是我，警探。”

En: Oh, great. She doesn't sound thrilled. "What else do you need, detective?"
Zh: “哦，太好了。”她听起来不是很高兴。“你还有什么需要的吗，警探？”

En: What do you mean by that?
Zh: “你这话是什么意思？”

En: You know... She seems to be looking for words. "What the Union says, goes. People listen to them and they take care of their own, which is, like, everyone here."
Zh: “你知道的……”她像是在搜寻词语。“工会说什么就是什么。大家都听他们的，而他们会照顾自己人，差不多就是，这里所有人吧。”

En: Legally, no. In reality, yes. He looks around. "Martinaise is *de facto* policed by the Dockworkers' Union."
Zh: “法律上不是的。但事实如此，”他看了看周围。“马丁内斯*实际上*是靠码头工人工会来维持治安的。”

En: The fucking nerves on her?! Trying to hide behind WEAKNESS. You're a GOD. An angry but JUST God.
Zh: 她神经紧张？！试着隐藏在脆弱背后。你是神。一个愤怒但却公正的神。

En: (Yell:) "I'M A GOD. YOU REPORT TO ME, NOT TO THE UNION -- IS IT CLEAR?"
Zh: （大喊：）“我是神。你应该向我汇报，而不是工会——明白了吗？”

En: This exact conversation has happened before. Establishing authority before this young girl seems to have been important to you in the past. Don't go there again.
Zh: 以前也发生过一模一样的对话。对于过去的你来说，在这个年轻女孩面前建立权威是很重要的事。别再回去了。

En: Wait, how did you first learn I'm a police officer?
Zh: “等等，你一开始怎么知道我是个警探的？”

En: You told me back in the Whirling... you told *everybody*. And showed us your badge... I don't need to hear about it anymore.
Zh: “你在飞旋旅社的时候告诉我的……你告诉*所有人*了。还把警徽拿出来给我们看……我不需要再听第二遍了。”

En: You were waving it around in everyone's face, begging them to describe it. You said it 'calms you'. And then you started making suicide jokes. It got pretty *graphic*.
Zh: “你当着所有人的面挥舞它，让大家形容它。你说它能‘让你冷静’。然后你就开始说些自杀的玩笑话。我又想起那个生动的*画面*了。”

En: And what? What did I do?
Zh: “然后呢？我做了什么？”

En: Actually, I don't want to know. I don't want to know what happened.
Zh: “其实呢，我不想知道。我不想知道发生了什么。”

En: Oh, those again? I have been trying to wean you off them.
Zh: 哦，又要来吗？吾辈一直试着帮您摆脱掉的。

En: Off of what?
Zh: 摆脱掉什么？

En: You know, when you put your gun, your *actual* gun, on your temple and pretend to shoot your brains out? Off of that. People don't like that.
Zh: 您知道的，当你掏出您的枪，我是说您*真正*的枪，对准太阳穴，装作想把脑袋打开花的时候？就是摆脱掉这个。大家不是很喜欢这样。

En: Hm, I remember this. You were screaming things like "My brains are all over the walls, painting them red. I won't be seeing it, cause these are my brains. I can't see without my brains!" Very nice visuals there.
Zh: 嗯，我记得这个。你大声嚷嚷着什么“我的脑浆会喷射在墙上，把墙面全部染红。我是看不到了，因为那是我的脑子。没有脑子我什么也看不到！”画面很形象。

En: Some poor sod was trying to eat his pudding, while you were screaming, spit flying, imitating the *mercy shot* right next to him... Spat some in his food, I don't think he touched it after...
Zh: 有个可怜的家伙想吃掉他的布丁，然后你开始大叫，吐口水，就在他旁边模仿*悲悯射击*……还朝他的食物吐了点口水，之后他好像就没碰过了……

En: People tried to back away from you or even slip out of the door, but you screamed: I AM THE GODDAMNED LAW AND YOU HAVE TO LISTEN TO ME, YOU ARE ALL SUSPECTS IN A MURDER INVESTIGATION!
Zh: 大家想离你远一点，甚至是溜出门外，但你开始大喊什么：“我他妈就是法律，你们都得听我的，你们都是一起凶杀案调查的嫌疑犯！”。

En: I should have killed myself...
Zh: “我真应该自杀……”

En: Why would I threaten to kill myself?! I mean, look at this world, I would love to stay!
Zh: “我为什么威胁说要自杀？！我是说，看看这个世界，我很愿意留下来！”

En: Yes, but what happened to my gun? (Just conclude.)
Zh: “是的，但是我的枪怎么了？”（总结。）

En: No, please, no more suicide threats! Thank *god* you don't have that stupid gun anymore...
Zh: “不，拜托，别再搞什么自杀威胁了！*谢天谢地*，你总算是没有那把愚蠢的枪了……”

En: Real policemen have uniforms too, by the way. Where's yours?
Zh: 话说回来，真正的警察都是有制服的。你的去哪了？

En: Yes, well... The static on the radio suggests Sylvie doesn't have a comment.
Zh: “是的，呃……”安静的电流声代表塞尔维对此没有任何评论。

En: Actually, I think I might be a birdie. A birdwatching enthusiast, you know.
Zh: “其实，我觉得自己可能是一只小鸟。你知道的，一个观鸟爱好者。”

En: Her voice reappears from the static: "That's wonderful, really. Maybe you should keep pursuing that hobby. Maybe it would calm you down a little."
Zh: 电流中再次响起她的声音：“那很好啊，真的。也许你应该继续追求这个爱好。也许这样能让你冷静一些。”

En: Okay, officer... Her voice is withdrawing as if she's moving away from the speaker.
Zh: “好吧，警官……”她的声音越来越小，就像是远离了听筒一样。

En: It was a pretty bird, there since I started working in the Whirling. I really liked her. We called her Scotty...
Zh: “那是只漂亮的小鸟，我刚开始在飞旋旅社工作的时候它就在那了。我真的很喜欢它。我们都叫它斯科蒂……”

En: Crackling static... more static and yes, more of that sweet static. The violence of your words is obviously scaring her.
Zh: 爆裂的电流声……更多电流声，没错，更多甜蜜的电流声。你言语里透露出的暴力显然是吓到她了。

En: Do you know how my paperwork ended up in the trash container behind the Whirling?
Zh: “你知道我的工作报告跑到飞旋旅社背后的垃圾箱里去了吗？”

En: Do you know who put the victim's clothes in the trash?
Zh: “你知道是谁把被害人的衣服丢进垃圾箱的吗？”

En: No, I don't know. It wasn't me... She gulps. "I haven't been out there since... I was terrified of the stench and the corpse." She falls silent.
Zh: “不，我不知道。不是我……”她有些喘不过气。“我一直没去过那边，自从……我很怕那股恶臭，还有那具尸体。”她陷入沉默。

En: Wait -- police documents?
Zh: “等等——警察档案？”

En: I must have had some sort of official police documents on me. Do you know where they could be?
Zh: “我身上一定带着某种官方的警察档案。你知道可能在哪吗？”

En: *I* am the authority around here.
Zh: “*我*才是这里的权威。”

En: Okay. She obviously doesn't want to challenge your authority. You feel *much* better now.
Zh: “好吧。”她显然不想挑战你的权威。现在你感觉*好多了*。

En: Wait, Kim, (Lower your microphone.) "Is she speaking truth? The Union is the law around here?"
Zh: “等等，金，”（放下你的话筒。）“她说的是实话吗？工会在这里执法吗？”

En: Words are not necessary to feel the lieutenant's discontent for the situation.
Zh: 警督对这种情况的不满，根本不需要用言语来表达。

En: What trouble?!
Zh: “什么麻烦？！”

En: Okay, I understand.
Zh: “好吧，我理解。”

En: You don't live here, you *don't* understand. Squealing is frowned upon here -- everything is dealt with... well, by the Union. Internally.
Zh: “你不住在这里，你*不*明白的。这里是不允许告密的——所有一切都是……呃，工会处理的。内部解决。”

En: You hear static. Her chin rub against the receiver. Then: "Please, I just didn't want any trouble."
Zh: 你听到电流的声音。她的下巴摩擦着听筒。接着：“拜托，我不想惹上其他人。”

En: Hey! The lieutenant interrupts with a flicker of anger in his eyes. "If you cannot restrain yourself then I'll be handling the interviews from now on and you can quietly take notes."
Zh: “喂！”警督打断了你的话，眼睛里带着一丝愤怒。“如果你控制不了自己，那从现在开始就交给我来询问，你在边上安静做笔记。”

En: You got your methods, I got mine lieutenant. Now let me do my job.
Zh: “警督，你有你的方法，我也有我的。现在，能让我完成我的工作吗？”

En: Sorry, I don't know what came over me, I just lost it -- I might have an anger issue.
Zh: “抱歉，我不知道是怎么回事，就是失控了——我可能有愤怒管理方面的问题。”

En: Apparently so. Please do try to control yourself. The anger is gone from his eyes and it's almost as if his voice is softer than before.
Zh: “显然是这样没错，请试着控制你自己。”怒气从他的眼中消散，他的声音好像都比之前柔和了。

En: Cop with an anger issue, underpaid, overworked and on the verge of collapse in both personal and professional life -- that's more of a rule than an exception. And the lieutenant knows this. He understands.
Zh: 有愤怒管理问题的警察，报酬过低，过度劳累，个人和职业生活都处在崩溃的边缘——这更像是一种惯例，而不是例外。警督也知道这一点。他能理解。

En: Breathing on the other end suggests that Sylvie is still on the line. She heard everything.
Zh: 电话那头传来的呼吸声说明塞尔维还没挂电话。她听到了一切。

En: Fine. The lieutenant clenches his jaw. "But know that I don't approve of such gratuitous volatility."
Zh: “好吧。”警督咬紧下巴。“但要知道，我不赞同这种没来由的大喊大叫。”

En: An angry cop refusing to face his demons and taking his frustration out on others -- just a badge and a gun away from those he's manhandling. A fast burning candle -- that's the side you just exposed to the lieutenant. He doesn't judge, has seen it too many times all ready -- you both have probably, but he takes a mental note.
Zh: 一个愤怒的警察，拒绝面对心中的恶魔，把自己的挫折发泄到其他人身上——只是因为一枚警徽和一把枪，就选择粗暴地对待其他人。一只快速燃烧的蜡烛——这就是你刚刚暴露在警督前的一面。他没有评判什么，因为已经见过太多太多——你们可能都曾有过，只是他选择记在心里。

En: People don't have the money to have the cables put in again. They use the Union's phone or the one on the coast.
Zh: “大家没钱让电线重新投入使用。他们用的都是工会的电话，或者是海岸边的那一台……”

En: Yeah, I think she got it. Good job!
Zh: 是啊，我想她明白了。干得漂亮！

En: I'm going to let it slide. I don't need answers for every little thing.
Zh: 我就顺其自然了。不是每一件小事我都需要得到答案。

En: Kim doesn't have a *uniform* and he seems real to me.
Zh: 金也没穿*制服*，而且在我看来他很自然。

En: Not my uniform too! God, I should really look into that.
Zh: 不是吧，连制服也不见了！老天，我真的应该好好调查一下。

En: Right. It's better to not ask too many questions, lest people start thinking you're a *cop* or something.
Zh: 没错。最好别问太多问题，免得大家开始觉得你是个*警察*或者什么之类的。

En: There are officers who wear the signature Perseus Black uniforms to the highest ranks in the RCM and end up buried in them as well -- others do it more *casually*. Looks like you're one of them.
Zh: 有些穿着标志性珀尔修斯黑制服的警官，在RCM一路爬到很高的位置，最后还是穿着它下葬了——其他人更*随意*一些。看来你也是其中之一。

En: Somewhere in a dank rental apartment, two police officers stand in the dark. Trash and liquor bottles cover the floor. Things are not good.
Zh: 在一间潮湿的出租公寓里，两名警官站在黑暗中。地板上铺满了垃圾和酒瓶。情况好像有些糟糕。

En: Yes, you absolutely should. It's awful that you lost it.
Zh: 是的，你绝对应该这么做。如果弄丢就太可怕了。

En: He's in plainclothes. Voluntarily. It's different from not knowing where your uniform is.
Zh: 他穿着便衣。这完全是出于个人意识，这跟不知道你的制服在哪是不同的。

En: Great. There's relief in her voice. "Anything else?"
Zh: “很好。”她的声音有些宽慰。“还有别的事吗？”

En: There is silence on the other end of the line.
Zh: 电话线的那头一片沉默。

En: One of the men, trying not to slip on old newspapers, makes his way to the window and pulls the curtains apart. Roaches scatter as the light comes in.
Zh: 其中一个人，尽量躲开了脚下的旧报纸，走到窗边，拉开窗帘。阳光照射进来，蟑螂开始四下逃窜。

En: Goddamn... officer Michel 'Elfboy' Williams speaks to himself. His partner, Sundance Fischer, looks at the patrol uniform he's wearing -- then at an identical suit framed on the wall. It's blue and covered in dust. "Let's get the fuck out of here," he turns to Williams, "he hasn't been here in days."
Zh: “见鬼……”‘精灵男孩’米歇尔·威廉姆斯警官自言自语到。他的搭档圣丹斯·费舍尔看着他身上的巡逻制服——然后看向墙上挂着的那套一模一样的衣服。它是蓝色的，上面布满灰尘。“他妈的，我们赶快出去吧，”他转向威廉姆斯，“他已经几天没来过这里了。”

En: There's genuine sadness in her voice.
Zh: 她的声音里流露出真实的悲伤。

En: I've had sailors fighting, Union guys grabbing my ass, kids stealing booze. Once a guy was glued to the karaoke machine every night for two months. But *you*... She pauses.
Zh: “我见过水手打架，工会的家伙偷摸我的屁股，还有小孩来偷酒。曾经有整整两个月，有个家伙每天晚上都黏在卡拉OK机上。但是*你*……”她停了下来。

En: Hold on, which song?
Zh: “等等，什么歌？”

En: 'We Go On' by the OO. She sighs. "I can't listen to it anymore, you've turned it into a parody."
Zh: “招摇乐团的‘继续前行’。”她叹了口气。“我再也听不下去了，你把它变成了一场模仿秀。”

En: I, ugh... the ones I had to wrench out of your toilet.
Zh: “我……呃……那些档案是我从你的马桶里捡回来的。”

En: What happened to them?
Zh: “它们怎么了？”

En: I... dammit, I don't remember what I did to your damn papers! I don't remember every little thing I do. Resentment gives way to concern in her voice.
Zh: “我……见鬼，我不记得对你那该死的文件做了什么！不是每件小事我都会记得的。”她声音里的愤怒为担心让了路。

En: She's worried she might have accidentally done something bad. Then she remembers it's *you*.
Zh: 她担心自己可能会不小心做了什么坏事。然后她记起来是*你*。

En: Anything else I can help you with, officer?
Zh: “还有什么能帮你的吗，警官？”

En: You hear a sigh of relief on the other end of the radio. Wordless, the call breaks. Then the already familiar voice:
Zh: 你听见无线电那头传来一阵放松的叹息声。一阵沉默后，电话中断了。紧接着，那个早已熟悉的声音响起：

En: Right, thank you for talking with me. Take care. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，谢谢你愿意跟我谈话。保重。”[离开。]

En: Well. There's an uncomfortable pause. "You tried to jam it down the toilet, sir, clogging it completely. After I had *unclogged* the toilet and retrieved the paperwork I threw it out in the trash, thinking you didn't need it."
Zh: “呃。”一阵令人不安的停顿。“你试着把它塞进马桶，先生，完全堵住了。我把厕所*疏通*，找回文件之后，就把它们丢进了垃圾箱，我以为你已经不需要了。”

En: I am sorry about that. She doesn't sound like she's actually that sorry. "Anything else, detective?"
Zh: “我很抱歉。”听起来她不是真的抱歉。“还有什么事吗，警探？”

En: There are *way* more inventive ways than a gun to leave this world.
Zh: 要离开这个世界，我肯定会有很多比用枪更有创意的*方法*。

En: Call was terminated by the other party. Anything else, officer?
Zh: “对方终止了通话。还有其他事吗，警官？”

En: You mean why did I leave the bar? You can hear her tense up on the other side. "Honestly, I'm... not really comfortable discussing it with you, sir."
Zh: “你是说我为什么会离开酒吧？”你能听出另一头她的声音有些紧张。“老实说，我觉得……跟你讨论这件事不太舒服，先生。”

En: Wait, why aren't you comfortable discussing it with me?
Zh: “等等，为什么跟我讨论会不太舒服啊？”

En: Okay. Did you leave because of Garte?
Zh: “好吧。你离开是因为加尔特吗？”

En: Alright, I won't push you on this. Are you ever coming back to work? (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，我不会逼你。你还打算回来工作吗？”（总结。）

En: I, uh... The static wavers. "Let's just say I left because I needed to get away from someone."
Zh: “我，呃……”对面的声音摇摆不定。“这么说吧，我离开是因为我需要远离某个人。”

En: Get away from whom?
Zh: “远离谁？”

En: You know whom.
Zh: “你知道是谁。”

En: What? She stops abruptly. "No, why would you even think that?!"
Zh: “什么？”她突然停了下来。“不是，你为什么会这么想？！”

En: You think you hear a sliver of accusation in her words.
Zh: 你在她的话语里听到一丝谴责的意味。

En: Sylvie, don't be afraid of that pig. You have to stand up for yourself!
Zh: “塞尔维，别害怕那头猪。你必须为自己挺身而出！”

En: *Please* don't bring Garte into this, it's none of your business!
Zh: “*请*不要把加尔特卷进来，这跟你一点关系都没有！”

En: I already said I don't want to talk about this! You're messing everything up again!
Zh: “我已经说过了，我不想谈这个！你又把一切都搞砸了！”

En: Don't be paranoid, she's obviously talking about someone else, not you.
Zh: 别这么偏执，她显然说的是别人，不是你。

En: Maybe, I don't know... She seems to relax a little bit. "I just know I have to take some time off right now."
Zh: “也许吧，我不知道……”她好像放松了一点。“我只知道现在要放松一段时间。”

En: Yes, obviously. You were the worst client I've ever seen. And I have seen *so many* assholes in this place...
Zh: “显然是这样。你是我见过的最糟糕的客人。而且我本来已经在这里见过*够多*混帐东西了……”

En: But... when I spoke to Garte it seemed like he thought you left because of *him*.
Zh: “但是……我跟加尔特聊的时候，感觉他以为你离开是因为*他*。”

En: Sounds intense.
Zh: “听起来很激烈。”

En: That's it? That's not so bad.
Zh: “就这些？听起来也没多糟糕嘛。”

En: And *then* I had to deal with your toilet. The one you clogged with *police documents*, causing water damage downstairs in the kitchen.
Zh: “*而且*，我还要处理你的卫生间。就是你用*警察档案*堵住的那个，楼下的厨房都因此积水了。”

En: What does this Union have to do with anything?
Zh: “那工会跟这个又有什么关系？”

En: Wait, really? No, this is absolutely not true. I like Garte, I really do.
Zh: “等等，真的吗？不，肯定不是真的。我喜欢加尔特，真的。”

En: Didn't he cross a line when he asked you out?
Zh: “他邀请你出去的时候，难道没有越界吗？”

En: No, I was actually flattered, I've always liked him. It was just bad timing... with the corpse and all that.
Zh: “没有，我真的很开心，我一直很喜欢他。只是时机不对……那具尸体，还有其他的事。”

En: I didn't know *what* to say to him later. Then you came and destroyed the place. So I left without explaining. I should have told him maybe...
Zh: “我不知道以后该跟他说*什么*。然后你来了，毁了这个地方。所以我没做任何解释就离开了。也许我应该告诉他的……”

En: There's a pause. You can almost see her on the other side, the telephone cord coiled around her index.
Zh: 电话那头突然停了下来。你几乎能看见另一端的她，用食指缠绕着电话线。

En: I can tell him. (Accept.)
Zh: “我可以告诉他。”（接受。）

En: Understood. (Refuse task)
Zh: “明白。”（拒绝任务）

En: Right. Well... Are we almost done now, officer?
Zh: “好吧。那……我们可以结束了吗，警官？”

En: Okay, but... please don't mess it up. Please don't take out your gun or something.
Zh: “好吧，但是……请别再惹麻烦了。不要掏出你的枪，或者其他任何东西。”

En: There's no reply from the other end of the line.
Zh: 电话线另一头没有回应。

En: Huh, I don't even know what to say...
Zh: “嗯，我不知道该说什么……”
